content: "The Galaxy changes, and we must change with it." -Emperor Valkourion

The cockpit of Antioch bleeps at 0600, as Lexi is too exhausted and asleep, Wraith rises to speak with Hangar Control. The Sith requests clearance for takeoff, invoking Thrawn's name, and is granted a takeoff window. At 0800, 2 new arrivals approach the Antioch and Wraith opens the landing ramp, permitting them to come aboard. A Mandalorian and a Droid march up the boarding ramp. All the while Lexi has been tuckered out and recovering, Wraith specifically requests no one to bother the Jedi to permit her to recuperate fully.  The 2 new arrivals settle in: the Droid is in the Navigation Section, and the Mandalorian has settled into the Ready Room.
After a full 12 hours of sleep, Lexi awakens to find Wraith having made breakfast and approaching with a tray of appetizing foods.
**"Morning, feeling better I hope?"**

Lexi looks at Wraith bleary-eyed and props herself up against the headboard. "**Is it morning?**" She looks around for a chronometer to check the time for herself. In her exhaustion she'd lost all track of time.

“**Feeling better from sleep,**” she says in response to his question and eyes the tray he's carrying. “**Breakfast in bed?**” She asks lightly.
**"Not exactly no... It's actually past mid-day, we've got two new crew members I'd like you to meet... once you've eaten, showered, and dressed. I insisted that you were not disturbed."**

Lexi nods appreciatively as she looks over the contents of the tray. "**I told you I felt I could sleep for a week,**" she jests not surprised she had slept most of the day. "**If Upsilon-Three and the droid are here then I imagine we've left the Chimaera as well? What did you set our destination for?**"

**"We're still performing engine tests and bringing all systems fully online. Shaking off the dust so to speak. Chimaera's Sensors Officers are helping us with calibrating our sensors. We should be ready to depart shortly. I suggest to use this time to introduce yourself to our new crew, they'll want to meet the Captain after all."**
**"We're currently located about 50 miles off the Chimaera's starboard bow, attemting several acceleration tests."**

Lexi takes bites of the food as she listens, her appetite lacking even after the long sleep. "**Attempting?**" She asks, not liking the sounds that perhaps their new to them ship was going to need additional work. 
Taking a final bite, enough that she's hoped to satisfy Wraith's expectations, she wraps the loose sheet toghter around her shoulders and tosses her legs over the side of the bed. After a moment of dizziness from having been prone for so long she goes to her locker to retrieve a clean set of clothes. "**Once all the tests and calibrations are complete there's still one thing: our destination. **"

**"It's just protocol, the ship is actually performing beyond listed specs. Nozzle is having to upwardly revise the power parameters, even. Whoever owned this ship before, did a serious low-key glow-up."**
**"Regarding our first Destination, I haven't really given it much thought. You're supposed to be the Chosen One right? We'll go wherever you say."**

Lexi winces and turns. "**I was wondering how much you remembered. You know all about it now: the World Between Worlds and the Prophecy?**" She doesn't wait for him to answer as she gives a small shrug. "**While your vote of confidence is inspiring, I still haven't a clue where we should go.**" She leans against the locker as she considers. "**I have a recording of the portal showing when the clone will go into the past. Maybe something there can give us clues to where he'll at least travel from. I also have a tome with the locations of other temples, which may be suitable focal points for portals. It could be possible Palpatine will use one of these places.**"

**"I see... Well, I suppose its a start. If we need more information, we could head for the nearest Cantina too. Borealis Sanctum, the nearby Pirate Spaceport, is a few hours Coreward from our current coordinates."**
According to the Tome, the nearest location to you with a possible Temple is Dathomir.
Reviewing the recording doesn't yield any additional clues.
Lexi raises an eyebrow at the suggestion. "**A pirate spaceport? I can't imagine they're very welcoming towards newcomers.**"
Nearest confirmed Temple is on Dantooine.
**"Our Transponder Tag can be adjusted to read as a pirate vessel, in which case we could dock and play the role of independent underworlders if we so wished."**
**"Well, *I* can in any case, can't speak for you Miss Goodie Noshoes."**
Grinning, he adds: **"I could give you lessons."**

"**Maybe I'd just leave you to do the talking if you feel so confident about it and learn from your example.**" She ignores his jibe and the sudden warmth in her belly at his suggestion.
She decides to change the topic. "**While I get cleaned up would you pull information on Dathomir and Dantooine from the Chimaera's library for me please?**"
**"I'll get on it, meet me in the Cockpit after you've cleaned up and made ready to present yourself as Captain."**
Stopping himself, he raises an eyebrow and looks at her with a new expression: **"Unless you require some more... personal assistance?"**

Believing they are in agreement she was in the middle of nodding and getting ready to head for the fresher when his last query makes her stand still and then back to him. She examines his face, trying to decipher where his thoughts were leading him. Butterflies form in her stomach as her own gaze meets his. "**We've been down this road twice now,**" she says slowly.
**"I--I'm sorry, you're right. Forget I said anything."**
Wraith grits his teeth and turns away, not wanting her to see the expression on his face, wishing he had his mask about now.

Lexi winces as he turns away. "**While you're offering to help, I will need some help with the ties when I get dressed... My fingers still don't want to work right.**" She holds out her hand and shows how they're moving stiff and not closing all the way. The act also shows the deep bruising around her wrists. "**It's going to take a day or two in a Trance for the ligaments to heal,**" she explains matter of factly, her tone non accusatory. She glances over in the direction of where the rest of the crew waited. "**It would be for the best if what happened last night stays between us.**"
Wraith doesn't turn around, but nods, before heading off without a word.

Having showered and dressed, twenty minutes later, the crew of the Antioch meets on the Bridge. At the controls, there appears to be a new bulky Mandalorian in full Beskar Plate. His helm is Sinister and he is gripping the yolk with one hand, while working various switches on the switchboard on the ceiling above. At the Nav Station, a new humanoid droid is busy browsing through Starcharts and several planets are rotating on the Holodisplay, you recognize Dathomir by its red hue and Dantooine by its forest gray drab interspersed by bodies of water. Wraith stands in the middle of the cockpit, his arms crossed and folded, evidently playing the role of Imperial Commander, his Death Mask once again covering his mouth and nose.

Glad to be clean once more, Lexi is glad for the concealment her loose fitting robes offer her. With any luck no one would be the wiser she had sustained more injuries following her initial conflict with Wraith. 
As she enters the cockpit she looks at their newest arrivals and then at Wraith who was now wearing his mask, making note of what color his eyes have reverted to. She moves to stand next to him and observes the going ons.

Lexi observes his eyes have remained Cyan, though his facial expression was outwardly hostile, until stepping up just then, Lexi saw the lines at the corners of his eyes relax visibly at her arrival. Unfolding his arms, he clicks his heels and announces:
**"Captain on Deck."**

The Cockpit was organized in an old fashioned manner. At the center was a raised platform, no seats but grav-boots could be maglocked if needed. Around the room, various stations, mostly unmanned, were scattered. The forward section of course had the pilot seat and copilot seat, the former occupied by the Mandalorian.

The Mandalorian instinctively shoots up at Wraith's announcement, as if hoisted up by his underpants. Turning around and recognizing Lexi, he relaxes visibly and executes a lazy salute. **"Sergeant Breggor Kryze, reporting for duty, ma'am. I believe you already know me by my Codename, Upsilon Zero Zero Three."**

Lexi keeps a straight face at the Mandalorian's instinctive reaction at Wraith's call. "**Indeed, I think I'd like to dispense with military formalities if that's alright with you?**" She glances over at Wraith who seemed to have taken it up on himself to designate her as the one in charge. 
"**I was glad it was you Admiral Thrawn assigned to the task. I hope you weren't disappointed to be sent to help us.**" She intentionally leaves out the fact he would be Thrawn's spy.
**"Fine by me. S'been a while since I had to do wetwork. Any opportunity to jump ship is always welcome. Predictable regimented Navy life'll make ya weak... uh, no offense Captain."**
Lexi smiles at him amused, "**Yes, I could see where that might be the case,**" she motions at the controls. "**Is everything in order?**"
**"Uh...Yeah I guess so. Just waiting to input Nav Coordinates... Speaking of which..."**

The new Droid unplugs himself from the Nav Station, swiveling to clank in through the door to the Cockpit. **"Declarative Statement: Salutations, Captain. Warm Introductions: I am NZ-11, Advanced Field Intelligence Prototype. It is a pleasure to make your acquaintance.”**
**"We call it Nozzle."**

Lexi glances at Wraith now understanding the reference earlier. "**Right.. Nozzle.**" She looks the droid over, this was perhaps the closest she'd been to one. "**Likewise. Did I see you had pulled up information on Dathomir and Dantooine?**"

At Breggor's remark, the Droid had swiveled its head towards the ex-Mandalorian: **"Interrogatory Correction: I prefer the designation N-Zee."** Without waiting for a reply, it returns to looking at Lexi, photoreceptors whirring as its dual head cameras refocus. **"Warm Affirmation: Yes, Captain. I have memorized all relevant details, if you have any questions for me."**
"**Ok Nzee, well lets start with broad strokes then. What's the current status of the planets like their political affiliations.**"
The droid emits a few mechanical clicks and begins to speed-read a table of data:
https://docs.google.com/document/d/13TOfBFeW9s_q-BbHmqRDhaO-aG4W_fG2J2u2witJfnc/edit?usp=sharing

embeds.title: WOP Open World
embeds.description: Dantooine Astrographical information Region Outer Rim Territories[1] New Territories[2] Sector Raioballo sector[3] System Dantooine system[4] Suns 1: Dina[2] Orbital position 4[2] Moons 2[2] Grid square L-4[5] Trade routes Myto's Arrow[3] Veragi Trade Route[2] Rotation period 25 standard hours[2]...

content: He finishes reciting the raw data, then at the end adds with his normal cadence: **"General Conclusion: Dantooine is a world overrun by nature, wilderness, sporadic settlements and Jedi ruins, while Dathomir is a mostly abandoned planet steeped in the traditions of the Dark Side, rumored to hold secrets of Sith Sorcery. All are nominally under the control of the Galactic Empire, though neither makes enough credits to be worth taxing."**
**"Interesting. It seems either planet could be the site of Mount Tantis, theoretically."**

All things considered, she didn't like the sounds of Dathomir, particularly since she already had a Sith with her with control issues. Dantooine seems a safe option to see what they could find regarding the portals and the ceremony involved in time travel. "**We'll go to Dantooine first,**" she decides. "**Have all our provisions been transferred aboard?**" She asks  looking between Kryze and Wraith.
He clears his throat, **"Approximately 91% of our requisitions have been fulfilled, the rest were backlogged and non-critical, so in short, yes, Captain."**
**"Dantooine eh? I suppose that's far better'n the alternative. I'd hate to run into some kinda sexy Sith Witch. Uhh... again, no offense, Captain."**
**"Sergeant, the Captain has declared her preference for informalities, *not* vulgarities."**
**"Sir yes sir."** He gives a strong salute in affirmation.
**"Command Aknowledged: I shall begin to plot calculations for a Jump to the Dantooine System."**

Lexi resists the urge to cast a sideways glance at Wraith. Instead she purses her lips together. "**We may end up going to Dathomir in the future. We should prepare for the inevitability of coming across more Sith.**"
**"Yes, Captain. I will endeavor to enthusiastically prepare myself for combat against a Sith opponent."** He shifts his gaze directly at Wraith.
Wraith's eyes flare yellow at the implied threat. **"Mortals do not fare well against Sith in open battle, remember that, Sergeant, if you wish to safeguard your life."**

**"All respect due, Lieutenant, I'll see it fit to uphold the interests of the True Empire and the Orders of Grand Admiral Thrawn above all other priorities, including open battle with an egotistical Sith, if needs be."**
**"An honorable answer, from a man donning honorable armor. However, I find that individuals tend to compensate on the outside for what they direly lack within."**
"**That's enough,**" Lexi interjects.
Both men stop and gaze back at the Captain.
"**Regardless for our reasons for being here, we're working towards the same goals. You two don't have to like each other, but don't forget why we're here.**"
**"Yes ma'am."** Both say in unison.
**"Sardonic Interrogatory: Apologies for interrupting this vital team meeting, Nav Coordinates have been programmed into the Hyperdrive and await only your orders, Captain."**
Lexi looks between the two of them for a moment before turning back to NZ and giving a nod. "**Let's say our goodbyes to the fleet and get out on our way.**"
**"Order Acknowledged: Yes, Captain, patching through to the Chimaera's Bridge now."**
**"Greetings, Lieutenant-- Or, should I say, Captain. My Fleet Commander informs me you'll be departing shortly."**
"**Admiral...**" She starts in greeting. "**That's correct. We're preparing to depart for our jump coordinates.**"
**"It's best to not reveal over open space comms exactly where you're headed. Nevertheless, I wanted to personally tell you. Good Hunting."**

"**Thank you, Admiral. We'll be in touch. Antioch out.**" After motioning for the transmission to be cut, she looks at Kryze in the pilot's seat and NZ. "**What is our ETA to Dantooine once in hyperspace?**" She asks NZ.
**"Navigational Computation: 37 hours, Captain."**
**"Oh yeah, let's make this kitty purr."** He smacks the switch board.
**"All hands brace for Hyperspace. On your mark, Captain."**
He grasps a handle on the ceiling and pulls it downwardly, presenting it for the slightly shorter Captain.
**"Ceiling mounted Hyperdrive engage, Captain."**

Lexi feels some relief at the duration of the trip. It would be plenty of time for her to  recover. A grin alights on her face as Kryze lets loose a bit. She glances over at Wraith and then takes the handhold presented to her. The jump into hyperspace was still her least favorite thing about space travel. "**Let's get going,**" she says and then takes a deep breath to preemptively calm herself.

The grasping handle is useful for both steering and stability. A switch flipped with a flick of the thumb disengages the lock on the grasping handle, turning it into a Hyperdrive Thrust Handle, which ignites the Hyperdrive Thrusters.

Disengaging the lock she gives the others a quick nod. With the ship in position she grips the thruster handle. "**Engaging thrusters.**" As instructed in the basic pilot course she'd been put through, she engages the hyperdrive. Crossing the dimensional threshold into hyperspace her stomach does its usual flip.

The Antioch's whole deck thrums with barely restrained power as Lexi flicks the switch, turning into a palpable and audible thrum with a slightly higher, but still very low pitch. The stars visible through the near-panoramic front view-screen extend into bright lines against the backdrop of space as it bends into another dimension from the first perspective.
The Bridge is now completely silent, except for quiet calming bleets from the switchboards. The Antioch hurtles through Hyperspace, stereoscopic lights dancing in all windows.

**"Hyperjump successful. All hands are relieved. Captain and I will be occupying the Living Quarters during ship's night. Kreez, you've volunteered to bunk in the Ready Room. Droid, you're to stay in the Cockpit and notify us immediately of any change of status. Dismissed."** He salutes.

Lexi face shows confusion as she hears the arrangements. Apparently they had already discussed the lack of private living arrangements and made decisions while she was sleeping. Slowly she nods while she lets go of the handle. "**It's going to be close living while we're here. It sounds like we're well on the way of figuring it out. You're going to be fine in the Ready Room, Kryze? I don't recall there being even a fold out in there.**"

**"Uh, yes ma'am. I moved one of the foldout beds into the Ready Room so I should be just peachy. I've slept days on the hard floors of brigs, this is just plain luxury in comparison... Just make sure that crazy droid doesn't activate the Assault Doors, or else I'll be suddenly both asleep and airborne, not an ideal combination."**
**"False Acknowledgement: Roger that, preparing to Activate Assault Doors during Sergeant Kryze rest cycle."**
"**Assault doors?**" Lexi looks between them for an explanation. "**One of the special features we've been blessed with I imagine?**"

**"Damned clanker, pull a stunt like that'd and I'll screw your head off and reattach it up your metal shitshaft!"** The Droid warbles mockingly as it disappears into the Nav Room, one large Mandalorian chasing it down.
**"Double Assault Doors, with 4 grappling cables to airlift Special Forces Teams in and out."**
Emerges from the other end of the cockpit emptyhanded. **"The hell that Droid suddenly disappear off to?"**

**"He's a slippery one, Sergeant. Apparently capable of unnaturally effective Stealth, on account of being some kind of fancy Prototype. Plus... that Helmet of yours is composed of about 90% blind spots."**

The Droid quietly crawls off of the ceiling by doing an unnatural backbend that would have fractured any normal man's spine. Entering the Cockpit behind Breggor, he intones with melancholy: **"Sarcastic Query: I live to serve, may I be of assistance?"**
Lexi raises an eyebrow at the explanation. "**Well, let's refrain from utilizing them until appropriate times, shall we?**" She says, addressing the droid.

**"Alright, like I said, everyone out, Droid you're on duty. Orders are to stay visible to crew at all times unless explicitly told otherwise. Breggor, you're to refrain from putting hands on the Droid, unless explicitly told otherwise. Violations shall be met with Swift Reaper Justice. Now then, everyone, dismissed."**
With grumbling, the humans all file out, leaving the despondent Droid to sit and stare at the bright hyperspace lightshow.
Lexi follows Wraith's directive and heads back the way she had come to the living quarters and heads for the galley, fully determined to find the necessary components to make tea.
Lexi is able to search the cupboards and find some exotic tea variety pack, supposedly sold only in the Unknown Regions.
Giving the tea a suspicious sniff, she sets about making the tea and finally relaxes into one of the couches in the living room, sifting through the tome for any more clues and drinking the tea.
Wraith leans up against one of the large support struts, arms folded, **"So, what are we hoping to find... on Dantooine."**
The Tome describes an Ancient Jedi Temple on Dantooine.
It's exact location is given by a set of coordinates written in the Tome.

Lexi holds up the tome to him to show him the information. "**Clues. If we don't find anything about Mount Tantis there, then I hope to at least learn more about the portals and the World Between Worlds.**"

He scoffs, **"Wait you mean to say, a dusty book is the reason we're full speed ahead to that empty hunk of dirt? I think I placed too much faith in your title, Chosen One. And here I thought you would have felt a Force Connection drawing you there or something... If its just detective work, then I'm afraid you're going to need a detective... Not a Prophet. "**

"**Our only other lead is going to a pirate spaceport and hoping to pick up something useful.**" Lexi let's the book snap closed and she's quiet for a long moment.  "**If this doesn't pan out anything then we'll find another port cantina.**"

**"Truth be told I'd prefer a Pirate Infested Cantina over a Predator Infested Jungle Planet, but I suppose both have their benefits. Let me guess your reasoning, being so attuned with Nature you're just playing to your strengths by going to the forested planet of Dantooine, aren't you?"**
**"Can't say I find any fault with your logic, but you're forgetting that ancient ruins will yield very little compared to living flesh and blood rumormongers."**
"**It sounded remarkably more appealing than a Dark Side laden wild planet in present company,**" she says slowly.
**"My heart would break if I had one left after last night."**
He turns away, face blushing. **"Uh, never mind."**

"**I'm sorry.**" Lexi also looks away as her hands come to rest in her lap on top of the book. "**I didn't want to put you further into a position where you'd have to fight Dark impulses more than you needed to right now.**"
**"I'm not a Child. You don't need to protect me like one."**
**"If you come across some evil pirates, trust me, you'll be thankful to have a Dark Sider fully rage out all over them."**
Lexi is quiet for a moment. "**I know you're not a child, but you did put it to me to decide where we're going. The trip to Dantooine will give us both time to recover.**"
**"Shipwide Announcement: Attention, crew. Our Sensors are picking up a High Priority Distress Beacon in an adjacent planetary system. Intercept Course Deadline: 5 minutes."**

"**A distres call?**" Lexi looks at Wraith blankly for a moment before she's standing and heading for her locker to put the book away and head for the cockpit. "**So much for having time to recuperate,**" she complains under her breath.
Wraith follows her lead, as all four crew meet up in the Cockpit only 71 minutes since departing.
**"Situation Report, Nozzle."**

**"Sardonic Affirmative: Yes, Lieutenant Kai, we are reading a High Priority Distress Signal emanating from a nearby uninhabited system. In 4 minutes will be the optimal Intercept Point. We can either continue on to our Destination, or pull out of Hyperspace at the edge of the Beacon's transmit range. I am programmed to query the Captain regarding any status change, and have done so now."**
Lexi takes only a moment to think about it. "**Pull out of hyperspace at the edge of range. We'll see what we're dealing with and whether we can help.**"

**"Resigned Assent: Very well, Captain, as you wish, we shall waste precious resources on aiding total strangers, and rescuing their glacially failing meat bodies, which would have inevitably died anyway given enough time... I shall provide a 10 second countdown when we are nearing the Intercept Point, so that you're able to disengage the Thrusters."**

Lexi chooses to ignore the Droid's protests and moves over to the pilot's station. Looking between the two men she waits for one of them to protest their current course of action. "**I haven't had a chance to review the Antioch's full specs.  Do we have weaponry?**"
**"Double Laser Cannons, supposedly, but not fired in years."**
**"Additional Data: The diagnostics read weapons as functionary."**
**"Looks like the System Tests aren't over and done with just yet..."**
**"Permission to Boot up at the Gunnery Station?"**
He gestures one station over.
**"Intercept Point in 10... 9... 8.."**
"**You said the ship was performing extraordinarily well..**" she comments under her breath. "**Guess we'll see.**" More loudly she starts acclimating herself to the pilot controls. "**Yes, Kryze.**"
**"Settle into your Pilot legs, I'll disengage thrusters."** He jumps up to ready the ceiling mounted Hyperdrive lever.

**"All hands brace for real space re-entry."** Wraith pulls back on the Hyperdrive lever in time with NZ's countdown, and the lines coalesce into stars, spitting the Antioch out into the middle of an Asteroid Field dead ahead. **"Hyperjump complete, Captain."**
**"Picking up Distress Signal. Distance 440 miles, Heading 311."**

**"Sensors Report: Our Sensors are picking up the message in full, Captain. Seems that a Luxury Casino Starliner out of Coruscant has experienced engine failure amidst the debris field. They are requesting supplies including oxygen, food, and medical in return for a considerable cut of credits, based upon whether the creditors are able to survive the ordeal, to be paid upon transfer of certain well-off passengers to a safe harbor or vessel."**
**"Captain what are your orders?"**

Adjusted now to the console, she double checks the readouts and makes sure the shields have been brought up. 
"**Do our scans collaborate their status? Any sign they were attacked recently?**" Something regarding the ship isn't sitting right with her as she reviews the message. "**Let's do a lap around and make sure we're alone.**" She says, bringing up the thrusters and setting up a search arch as they approach the starliner. 

"**Open a channel, please,**" after waiting a moment for NZ to comply she responds. "**Starliner, the Antioch is ready to assist. What is your engine status?**"

**"Sensors Report: Scans indicate innumerable metallic signatures, likely asteroids. We are unable to pinpoint the exact location or status of the Starliner at this range, but we have the Distress Beacon's coordinates, inside the Asteroid Field."** Wraith adds: **"Most likely, we'll only have line of sight visuals once we're close enough to see past line-of-sight blocking asteroids."** As the Antioch does a lap around the periphery of the Asteroid Field, a new pinging is heard, to which NZ responds. **"Sensors Report: Captain, our Sensors are picking up new transient signals-- they've just disappeared again. Approximately 3 to 5 signatures, judging by the signal amplitude, single-person craft most probably."** NZ's Scomplink finger-connector whirrs and rotates as he continues to feed commands into the ship's computer. **"Acknowledged: Opening Comms."** The Channel opens and Lexi transmits her message, directed at the Distress Beacon. In return, a few seconds later, a weak static-laden voice of a clearly distressed human male responds: **"--arliner Royal Flush. We have experienced catastrophic engine failure, requesting assistance. Repeat, we are dead in the water, reques--"** The transmission dissolves into static. NZ intones, **"Communication Report: Captain, our Comms are being blocked by debris and magnetic asteroids."**

"**Of course,**" she comments idly as she brings the Antioch closer. "**Let's keep an eye out for those fighters.**" Coming in through a circuitous route she gives them time to track the movement of the asteroids and put better eyes on anything lurking in the shadow.

Approaching the Distress Beacon’s location, the Antioch has to do some fancy maneuvering to get out of the path of oncoming asteroids. Minor debris impacting burns off the Shields slightly, which remain above 95%. Approaching the densest cluster of the Asteroid field, the Starliner Royal Flush finally comes into view, as you crest over one of the larger asteroids. It appears to be an extremely corroded, damaged, and old battered frigate of indeterminable manufacture. 

All the while, a niggling feeling in the back of Lexi’s mind grows ever more strident, and all of a sudden her Force senses are outright screaming at her, much like they were when the Chimaera was ambushed at Myrkyr. The Feeling builds and all of a sudden alarms start blaring on the switchboard.

**“Engine Report: Captain, a tractor beam has us pinned down.”** The Antioch begins to decelerate, and Lexi has to wrestle with the controls with all her might, but thanks to her training is able to break the hold of the Tractor Beam from the Royal Flush, which mistakenly locks onto a stray Freighter-sized asteroid instead, finally releasing the Antioch, if only temporarily. Lexi had effectively flown the ship in such a manner as to confuse the tractor-beam operator into grabbing an asteroid instead.

During this period of time, from behind one of the Asteroids, four small single-man starfighters had broken out in a dead sprint, accelerating into an all out attack run on the Antioch. Fortunately, Lexi had the foresight and warning of the Force, and the pilot skill to escape the deadly trap. The Jedi even kept her wits about her enough to entirely avoid the newest Surprise Attack, by ducking the ship behind another large Asteroid. Red blaster cannon fire strafes the asteroid providing cover, missing entirely from the first pass.

Nevertheless, the 4 hostile starfighters quickly regroup, recover and re-engage once more by turning around and starting another Attack Run. Now that the surprise had worn off, it seemed this was going to come down to a straight up space battle.

The starfighters come on with lethal alacrity, red blaster cannons blazing. One line of deadly energy hits the Antioch squarely on its Shields, but the Freighter’s Defense Bubble holds solid, remaining at 72% readiness. It seems the refurbished Dynamic-class was tougher than her size suggested. In response, Breggor gave a radiant cry as one of his blaster cannons found its mark, **”Direct hit!”** Now down to 3, the starfighters had made a U-turn and were angling for another pass. Evidently, the Tractor Beam operator could barely keep up with the lightning-fast dogfight and his beam found nothing to grab on to. 

The next Attack Run pass was no less lethal, once again scoring a direct hit on the Shields, which were reading at 48% now. At this rate, the Antioch wasn’t going to last very long. Breggor’s return volley missed entirely, but Lexi at the Pilot station had one of the Starfighter flit across her sights just briefly. For an ordinary Pilot it would have been a fraction of a second, too short a time period to react, but Lexi was in the zone - time slowed briefly for her, perfectly in time to allow her to squeeze the trigger and blast another enemy starfighter to dust. She felt his life signature in the Force extinguish in that moment. 

Only 2 starfighters remained at this time, but they were determined to keep up their blitz. Turning for another pass, this time however, Lexi had fewer enemies to contend with and easily dodged their attacks. It seems that without force of numbers, and without the advantage of surprise, each individual fighter was much less of a threat now. Breggor gives another hoot of joy as his guns find their mark. **“One left!”** Wraith points, holding on to the ceiling handle, **“He’s had enough, he’s running.”** The battle was over barely before anyone could register what had occurred.

"**Unless he has a hyperdrive, he won't be going far,**" Lexi remarks as she maneuvers around the tractor beam range of the starliner. She frowns at the ship, not liking the idea of just leaving these pirates to their next victim. "**You may yet get to put some fear in some pirates.**" She says casting a glance back at Wraith. "**Let's get ready to board them.**"

"**Royal Flush, stand down or we'll open fire.**" She says over their comms channel to the baiting cruiser.

Until now the Royal Flush had acted dead in the water, and its engines didn’t appear to be operable. Now, all of that drastically changed. A volley of green beams fire and blast a couple of asteroids to chunks, these pieces flying in all directions to impact and destroy the lone remaining starfighter in a brilliant bright explosion. 

This time, an image is transmitted back across the Comm Channel. The visage was that of a new reptilian Alien, this creature has an enormous set of jowls.

**“Bl-This is Captain Bilbilov of the Royal Bl-Flush, blblblblbl.. Bl-We blahve you surrounded and in our blblblbl, trap! Make one blrong move and you blee OBLBLBLBLITERATED!”**

After the comms cut out in a hiss of angry static, Wraith hastily adds: **"That's a Hutt, likely part of a larger more powerful, Hutt Crime Family. In this Galaxy, such creatures are not to be destroyed without earning a Deathmark."**

"**Huh,**" Lexi says simply as she avoids the asteroids the Royal Flush had sent into motion. "**I've seen all I want to here... NZ, recalculate our jump and get us back on track.**" 
Maneuvering to avoid the Royal Flush's tractor beam, Lexi utilizes the asteroids to their advantage to avoid further conflict with the Hutt controlled ship.
Very soon, the Antioch jumps back into Hyperspace, leaving one angry Hutt cursing at empty space. The Antioch is brought back to normal readiness, it is now evening aboard.

Lexi is relaxing in the galley, shifting a morsel of food on her plate, lost in thought as she contemplates the day's events. "**Are these the sort of tricks all pirates play,**" she asks to no one in particular and not expecting an answer to her thoughts said aloud.

Wraith walked the Central dome of the Ship in lazy circles, long ago having removed his Death Mask, once he had secured the forward section of the ship for the night. **"The Entire Galaxy has turned into a pool of parasites feeding off each other, desperately clawing through fellow parasites at every opportunity, thanks to the state of Eternal Stagnation brought about by the Reborn Emperor and his Galactic Empire."** His eyes were unusually lit up this evening.

Lexi watches Wraith as he paces their area for a moment before she sets her fork aside. Her meal is mostly uneaten as she stands to clean up after her meal. "**The current Empire is just a continuation of the empire the True Emporer established, is it not? Wouldn't it be fair to say that even in nine years things may end up in a similar state?**"
Lexi uses the terms used in the Chimaera, but she hardly believes in the restoration of the original Palpatine or the True Empire.
**"To stand still is to die. This stagnant empire shall soon collapse in on itself. The True Empire would never allow itself to be reduced to this sad state."**
**"The Death of a King allows his Kingdom to be reshuffled, or adjusted in a new direction. Thanks to Palpatine's Fear of Death, that could never occur, thus leading to this Frozen Galaxy."**
**"New Leadership is required to Sheppard this Galaxy, only someone like Thrawn should have the right to take up the Mantle."**
"**The galaxy has known darkness for too long,**" she comments softly and then she looks up at him. "**Do you really believe that or is it the indoctrination from our basic training winning out?**"
**"Our training was what saved us this day."**
**"Simply extrapolate that, and the whole Galaxy shall be resurrected."**
He halts short, stopping. **"Our training and... You acting as Captain. Very effectively, I might add."**

Finding it hard to accept any sort of compliment just gives a small shrug. "**We were all doing what we needed to do. I was more piloting than giving orders.**" She pauses looking at him. "**I just wish we could have done more to prevent them from conning their next target.**"

He discards her concern with a wave of his hand dismissively. **"Even if we did somehow succeed in defeating that frigate, a single parasite would just be replaced by another, possibly worse parasite, in the pecking order. Ultimately, it was probably for the best, that we left his incompetent corpulent highness up there to fail at fishing for more do-gooders."**

**"However, I won't allow you to deflect the thrust of my statement. You were absolutely brilliant in your role as Captain, I've rarely seen a decisive person act so adroitly in such a role. Perhaps you *were* born for this..."**

She gives a small snort of laughter at his description of the Hutt. As his words trail off she raises an eyebrow at him. "**I suppose we all must bear the burdens cast on us, but to say I was born to this.**" She gives a small shrug. "**We Mortals do as we must,**" she says in a half jest.
**"Evidently so."**
She blinks at him as she finishes up her task. "**Everything alright? You're like a caged nexu testing his bars.**"
**"I'm fine, just... melancholic."**
Lexi nods slowly, not sure a melancholic Sith sounded  ideal. "**Was the pacing helping?**"
**"What was helping, has been talking to you."**

"**Alright then... **" Lexi collects her mug of tea and head into the living room and takes a seat sitting on one of the unoclaimed couches. "**If talking helps.. let's talk. Anything in particular on your mind?**"
**"I can think of one individual, currently occupying my mind."**
He kneels down to rub her feet as she sips tea.

She catches onto his meaning fairly quickly especially as he kneels in front of her and relieves her of her boots and begins rubbing her feet. "**And thinking of this person is making you melancholic?**"
**"Yes, you see this individual is someone I care about deeply, but cannot express feelings for."**
He gently continues massaging her feet and lower legs, crunching a few joints in the process to relieve pressure.

Lexi grips her mug as he continues his massage, and bites down on the urge to moan as he hits a tender spot. Instead she focuses her mind on his words. "**There's more than one way to share affections,**" she manages.
**"The deepest affections can be shared by only one, don't you think..."**
A shiver runs through her at the boldness of his words. "**I like to believe there's someone out there for each of us.**"
**"I like to believe, if you keep your head in the clouds too often, you'll forget those beneath your feet."**
He finishes massaging her feet and stands up. **"If you want to continue your Physical Therapy, you'll have to turn onto your front so I can massage your back."**

She eyes him sharply for a moment wondering if she should call him out on his comment. After a moments hesitation she sets her cup to the side and does as suggested. Turning her back to him, she spreads out on the couch on her front, her head cradled on one arm as she watches him. "**Are you calling yourself or your... paramour a dreamer?**"
**"Maybe a little of both."** He kneels down once more and begins rubbing her shoulder muscles and then works his way down her spine.

Lexi relaxes under the ministrations of his strong hands as they work through the thick fabric of her robes to her sore muscles. She’d kept her heavy tunic on while there was a chance for someone to see the dark purple bruises on her arms left from the evening before. As Wraith used his mask, which was conspicuously absent during his melancholy, she had used the layers of clothing to shield herself. A shield which was proving inadequate as he hits a cluster of muscles and her breath hitches in response. 

Her eyes close for a long moment as she collects her thoughts and then as his hands move down her spine Lexi’s eyes pop open. She goes back to watching him and when their twin cyan eyes meet she slowly asks, “**Are you afraid?**” It is a question she’s been asking herself and is still coming to terms with.

Having sufficiently tenderized her spine, his hands suddenly stopped at her question, she can feel his hands trembling with emotion as he contemplates his words, **"It would be more accurate to say, I want to be very *cautious* with how I approach this individual. I have hurt her before in my over-eagerness, and I still do not fully have control of my mental faculties due to Clone Psychosis. I am incapable of love in my current state, but that doesn't make the desire to be loved any less prevalent. On the contrary, I find it to be, stronger than ever before."**

Lexi lays still as his trembling hands rest in place on her back. Slowly she sits up until she’s nearly level with him. “**A person is never incapable of love. It’s not something we’re taught, but exists in every fiber of our being. What is taught is how to love; how we express and accept it in return.**” Tentatively, she reaches out and clasps one of his hands in both of hers.

Initially flinching at her touch, as if she had sent a current through his body, he is taken aback at her tender words. His eyes seemed to search her still gaze, darting back and forth until stopping entirely as he processes her meaning, at last meeting her gaze with a sense of resolve. **"Is it possible to learn this power?"** He feels her hands clasp over his, in a rather intimate gesture, and cannot help but shrink away from her confrontation, afraid of the pain he could inflict upon her fragile body if he lets his animal instincts loose once more.

Sensing his uneasiness at her forward touch she loosens her grip on his hands. Her gaze, however, remains resolute“**Yes, though it’s not something to be learned overnight or easily. It’s full of its own pain and rewards.**” She gives him a small, reassuring smile. “**There’s no instruction manual, it’s just something you learn with time.**”

**"In that case, I very much look forward to our... lessons."** He seems mesmerized, gaze unflinchingly meeting hers and letting silence hang in the air. Random muscles seem to twitch fractionally over his body, as if miming what he yearns to do to the Jedi in a more intimate setting.

Lexi feels her cheeks begin to flush under the intensity of his gaze. The silence hangs between them as she continues to clasp his hand in hers, her cool fingers warming quickly while in contact with his skin. With their close proximity, she has to restrain her own urges to reach out and touch him. She’d learned her lesson regarding bringing his passion to the forefront and the idea of doing so again frightened her. 
“**What now?**” she asks, her thumb unconsciously caressing the back of his hand.

**"Now? I was thinking we could try catching up on sleep, I hope you don't mind if I left standing orders for Distress Beacons to be disregarded during the night. You see, I want to try picking it up where we last left off in bed, if you're feeling bold?"**

Lexi's stomach flips with his suggestion. The desire to be close to him is still there, but wariness over the result of their attempt the night before was prevalent. "**Our desire got the better of us last night.**" She says slowly, her wariness evident. "**I don't think I could handle another encounter with your Darker self right now.**"

He nods, as if expecting her answer, **"Of course, I understand, I'll be patient with you, just promise me you'll use all of that Jedi wisdom, and approach me on your own initiative when you're ready to try intimacy again with me."**
She lets out a breath. "**Will there ever be intimacy? We can't seem to even kiss without it causing some sort of reaction.**"
Wraith shrugs, half smiling, **"The most powerful Force in the Universe."**
He collapses into a couch, kicking his feet up and crossing his arms behind his head, **"Well now that that's out of the way, we can kick back and relax with a nice evening."**

She's taken a back for a moment and flinches as he collapses onto the couch next to her jostling her in the process. "**And if NZ is holding calls... A relatively quiet evening.**" Grabbing her now cooled mug of tea she leans back on the couch,  bringing their bodies closer together. "**What do you like to do in your downtime?**" She asks, swirling the tea around.
**Mostly I like to get into misadventures with interesting new friends."**
**"Maybe we can stop by that pirate spaceport for a stiff drink?"**
"**You're disappointed we're going to Dantooine and not the spaceport.  We'll make it there. Or somewhere like it no doubt.**"
**A bit, yeah. I figured we'd finally left primitive lands behind. I guess a Nexu really can't change it's stripes..."**
**"So let's say we find that Jedi Temple from your Tome, what are we gonna do with it?"**
"**Depends on what we find there.**"Lexi gives a little shrug. "**Let the Force guide us.**"
Growing used to his presence beside her, she leans in to let her head rest against his shoulder.
**"So, how are you liking the crew so far?"**
"**Being as it's my first crew?**" She looks at him sideways with humor. "**I think they'll do. We seem to work well together.**"
**"Indeed, they seem to instinctively respect you and give you the proper deference."**
**"Alright, I suppose we'll have to Trust in the Force and let it guide us to the right destination. I just hate leaving my fate in the hands of some disembodied mystical energy field."**
"**You'll have to get used to it... I have the Force..  and you. to keep me straight in this galaxy.**"
**"I mean do you really think that such a thing can possibly know what is Right from Wrong."**
"**The Force itself has no concept of right and wrong. Only balance.**"
**"Balance between what then."**
"**Between chaos and stagnation. Fear and hope. The balance in ourselves when we can embrace our emotions and yet be at peace.**"
**"The longer I find myself inhabiting this Galaxy, the more I see purposeless hunger for resources for the sake of selfish desires. Not any kind of Balance."**
She grows quiet for a moment. "**When the scales are tipped, such as they are now, a champion is chosen. So says the Prophecy of the Chosen One at least.**"
**"Chosen to bring Balance to the Force, yes, I read of it from your mind... I hope it is a True Prophecy, and not just some Vain Hope of a dying Galaxy."**
"**Sometimes hope is all there is.**" She pauses for a moment and turns her head to blink at him  "**How often are you reading my thoughts?**"
He blushes, only now realizing she wasn't exactly cosigning this action. **"Only when you are connected to me by the Force. Most other times you are completely closed off to my spiritual presence."**
**"I wasn't trying to read your thoughts, it was more like... I was just receiving them from you."**
"**Through our bond forged by the World Between Worlds,**" she says thoughtfully. "**You've said the bond goes both ways, but I haven't experienced the same from you.**"
**"Are you saying you cannot read my innermost thoughts and feelings?"**

"**Not as you've seemed to have experienced from me. I only get impressions, much like I do with everyone else.**" She looks up at him. "**It doesn't bother me. It's probably for the best you know about it. Though I would have left out the Prophecy,**" she grimaces as she looks away and for a long moment she's quiet as she thinks on that day so many weeks ago. “**I was so angry when I realized they were sending me away over some words on a tablet. It wasn't even me they were originally looking for. Only when He had failed did the Force choose me.**"

**"Of course, I saw the name of the previous Chosen One, Anakin Skywalker. He was destined to Bring Balance to the Force, and was instead murdered by the Emperor Reborn. I sincerely hope the same fate doesn’t befall you…. doesn’t befall *us*."**

Lexi is relieved she doesn't have to explain further. He of course had already gleaned the information when their bond had been it's strongest. "**Us? Given up on trying to dissuade me from my unrealistic path?**" She asks with mild amusement, looking away from him she focuses on the cold tea in her hand.

**"Actually... I've been giving it some thought. It may not be as unrealistic as I initially estimated, but... I'm not ready to present my plan yet. I too have been racking my brain about how to defeat the Reaper Queen, and when I'm around you... I find my thoughts flow more clearly."** He seems pensive, internal at first. Then, as his speech flows he seems to reawaken and his eyes light up once more, shining a warm glowing cyan energy as he turns abruptly to look directly at her face. Lexi can almost feel the warmth from the glow of his eyes on her cheek.

"**My plan so far hasn't gone farther than trying to find Mount Tantis and learning more about the portals.**"Feeling his gaze on her, she turns to look up at him and raises an eyebrow in question. "**What is it?**"
**"You're not going to approve of this, but if you want to save the Galaxy you will have to utilize the Dark Side. To murder the murderers."**
His right eye glows a red hue briefly, before returning to cyan.
Her mouth opens in shock at his declaration. "**What's brought you to this conclusion?**" She asks at last.

**"How do you plan on saving the Galaxy, by talking your enemies to death? Preaching to them until they surrender? There is only one real way, to save our failing world, and we both know what it is."**
This time, the spark of red lasts for a full 2 seconds as he speaks, once again returning to cyan afterwards.
"**I'll fight...**" Lexi starts watching him as his inner demon surfaces. "**And I'll kill if I must, but to say it's the only way would be to forsake everything I am.**"

Wraith wanted to yell at her and shake her by the shoulders, to make her see. His red eye flared angrily, but outwardly he did none of these things. It would do no good. Instead he said: **"I will guard you and guide you, stay at your side, and give you every opportunity to find a way to save our world that doesn't involve betraying your Jedi Code, but know this... if I see for even an instant, that you might hesitate to save this Galaxy due to your stubborn morality, I will *not* hesitate to take matters into my own hands. At which point, you and I will be enemies once more, on Opposing Schools of Thought once more... just like where we started. I cannot permit your Weakness to lead to the Death of the Galaxy. I cannot permit... another Anakin Skywalker."**

"**Skywalker fell into Darkness when he let his fear and anger control him. He lost his balance and in doing so the scales were tipped. If you think fighting darkness with more darkness is what's going to save this galaxy, then you will be turning me into another failed Chosen One.**"

Right eye still grimacing and glowing an evil red, his left eye remained calm and had a pained expression, **"Perhaps that is what my role is here, to take on the Darkness and leave you standing in the Light."**
He turned away, closing both eyelids and shutting off his luminous gaze.
"**No!**" The word comes out sharply and she shakes her head vehemently as she gazes at him. "**That is a burden I won't place on you.**"
He remains motionless, eyes closed.
**"It is inevitable... Search your Feelings, you know this to be true."**
"**I know the future is uncertain,**" she says stubbornly.

**"No matter how vehemently you wish otherwise, Darkness nevertheless exists. The Jedi and Sith, are Two Parts of One Whole. One cannot have Love without Hate, Peace without War, Life without Death, Light without Darkness, or Justice without Punishment."**
He turns back to face her full on, right eye glowing brightly red while his left eye glows the softest warmest hue of cyan you've seen thus far.

Lexi is at a loss of words, unable to argue against his reasoning. Gazing into his eyes, the embodiments of Wraith's dual personas, she gently reaches up and cups the right side of his face. "**We'll walk this path together and bear whatever hardships are placed there. We are strong enough to overcome the Darkness.**"

Wraith's face swims out of view all of a sudden for Lexi, as touching him, she is struck in turn by a Force Vision. She surveys a Forested Planet, zooming down in quick bursts to reach a Cold Artificially-Carved Mountain range, the central Mountain being the largest by leaps and bounds. Flying/phasing into the Mountain, she sees a gallery of Cloning Tanks. Zooming into one of them, phasing through the glass, diving into the water, Lexi reaches with a ghostly hand to cup a familiar face: Wraith's face. Dual eyes open, shining red/cyan, and the vision fades returning Lexi to the Antioch touching Wraith's cheek.

Lexi blinks heavily as the vision clears, her expression only shifting slightly as she tries to process what it was she saw. Her fingers linger on his cheek for a moment before she lets her hand fall into her lap once more. "**We turned what was supposed to be an enjoyable evening into a debate,**" she says at last to ward off any questions.
His glow seems to diminish with her change of subject, both irises cyan. **"Discussing philosophy with you is perfectly enjoyable to me."**
One eyebrow raises, **"Unless you can suggest... some other way to pass the time."**
"**I think I saw a holochess table,**" Lexi says coyly.
**"Let's try it tomorrow, for tonight I think I'll turn in."**
"**Probably for the best,**" she says. After pausing for a moment, Lexi seems to come to a decision. "**We could try sharing the bed again.**"
**"I'll need to hit the fresher and shower first. As always, you're free to join me. I hide nothing from my friends."**
Standing, he removes his clothing and throws it on the floor, stalking off to wash his body.

"**Oh... I noticed**" Her cheeks turn pink and she tries not to watch as he saunters to the refresher. As he disappears, she bends down to collect his discarded clothing and throws them into the washer, adding her own clothing to fill out the load. Not quite brave enough to join Wraith in the shower, she grabs a change of her night clothes from her locker and heads into the private shower to get cleaned up herself.
Returning after having cleaned up, Wraith dives into the bed to begin warming it up

Lexi pads barefoot into view wearing her usual tank top and a pair of loose pants to sleep in. After a moment of hesitation, she lifts the covers slightly and gets under the warmth of the covers. Wraith's heat already seemed to be warming up the bed nicely.  "**Do you always run  so hot?**" She asks, shivering as her body adjusts to the sudden warmth.
Wraith opens one eye, lazily smiling, **"You call this warm? You should see me when I exert myself."**
Opening the sheets, he allows her room to enter and lay nearby, Lexi immediately feels his warmth radiating from his body like a fireplace.

He allows her to adjust herself however she likes, not wanting to make any more erotic advances, and trying to fall asleep before she can trigger his animal instincts. He closes his left eye once more, but to him sleep still wouldn't come. After a few minutes, he decides to open his left eye to see how Lexi is getting on.

Lexi has turned to lay to where she's half laying on her side facing him and on her stomach, a pillow supporting her head and body. She's calmed her breathing, but doesn't seem to be asleep which is evident as she too opens an eye to gaze at him.  "**This brings back memories,**" she says lightly.  "**That first night sleeping across from each other around the fire.  Though it wasn't nearly as warm or comfortable.**"
Wraith's other eye opens, as he looks her up and down. **"How could I forget, the terrified but brave Jedi girl who I spared on a whim, has grown into a fine and mature woman."**
"**Spared on a whim? That's not how I remember it.**"
**"Fair. In a way, you also saved me just as I spared you. Who knows what could have been, had I succeeded in my mission, I would be lost now, without you..."**

"**You'd have killed me only to have been eaten by Vornskrs,**" she muses and then she gazes at him with all seriousness. "**I'd be lost, too...**" She gives him a small smile as she tries to leven the conversation. "**Breggor doesn't seem the philosophical type. He's probably a dull conversationalist.**"
**"I've survived worse... the Dark Side is a powerful ally. It simply requires... an even temperament. Can you imagine the power if our Schools were ever to combine..."**
He lays down flat on his back, scooting closer to her to gaze at her face from below, getting a better view of her might put his mind at ease and permit him to drift off to sleep after all.

"**It would be an interesting exercise to reconcile the differing philosophies,**" she says before covering her mouth to hide a yawn. Letting out a breath she settles her head on the pillow. "**I'm going to sleep in a Trance tonight,**" she says as a warning. "**It will be the only way I'll be healed in time.**"
Nods. **"I'll protect you with my life."**

Lexi gives Wraith a small smile at his promise. Shifting slightly she brings up the blanket till it reaches her ears and she snuggles further into the warmth it holds in. With the ease that comes with years of practice, she calms her mind and body. Her breathing and heartbeat slow to be imperceptible as the Force envelopes her in its sustaining embrace. It’s in this dreamless state she finds the peace for her mind and body to heal at last. 

When Lexi wakes, she has no concept of how long she’s been in the Trance. Turning slowly onto her back she begins to slowly stretch out each of her muscles starting with her feet and working her way up. In the dim light she looks at her wrists and sees the angry bruises from the day before were now gone. The ligaments in her wrists were still stiff, but no longer pain her.

Wraith's sleep was listless. He had tossed and turned, large body tussling around under the sheets, bumping and distorting Lexi's anatomy all through the night, although neither were conscious of such ongoings while resting.

Lexi turns back to her side to face Wraith and pulls up the blanket against the chill air of the ship. The sheets, she notices, are in disarray around them. She watches her bed partner for a long moment.
Her focusing in on Wraith consciously seemed to tug at their Force bond, and he began to stir awake at this signal.

Upon realizing her focus is waking him, she closes her eyes in an attempt to not disturb him further.  With the damage already done, she opens one eye and peers at him. "**I'm sorry...**" Her tone is soft and apologetic. ""*I didn't mean to wake you.**"
Darting upright, he awoke, eyes piercing her with his intensity. Scarlet intensity.
The bright scarlet gaze fades away as in his face your read recognition.
His face smoothes out, calming as he rises to a sitting pose. **"No apology required... In fact, you should wake up next to me, more often."**

Lexi doesn’t dare to move as his crimson gaze falls on her, but she prepares to move should things turn for the worse. As the shadow retreats and recognition of his surroundings returns she relaxes once more. She grabs at the sheet to hold it to her as he moves to sit up, not quite ready to relinquish it. “**Were you having bad dreams? You woke up with a dark expression.**”
**"That's what I love about your naïveté... so innocent and pure... The Dreams of a Sith are always Dark Ones. All the more thanks go to you, that you tugged me back to this dimension."**

Lexi is lost for words as he explains. Instead she sits up and leans back against the headboard so they are once more on an even plane. "**Do also tend to sleep so listlessly?**" She asks, indicating the state of the bed.
Gazing around the bed, he seems not to mind. **"That's how we do all our best dreaming."**
Returning his gaze onto her, he begins to stretch instinctively, not liking to remain still, but also not wishing to disturb the tenderness of the moment, wishing it could last a while longer.
**"So... it seems we have a day to kill until we arrive at our destination. How would you like to spend it?"**
Lexi seems to think on it for a moment. "**Does there have to be a plan? It's been a long time since I've had a day that wasn't regimented. Sometimes it's nice to just let things happen.**"

He thins his lips, smiling, as if to say *"Yep, that about tracks for what an Animal-taming Jedi Princess would say."* Instead he says: **"I see your wounds healed overnight, this must be the Jedi Force Trance you mentioned."**

Her lips purse in response to his obvious line of thinking but is glad when he moves on. "**Yes. I'm feeling much better.**" She straightens the blanket around her lap and let's her hands fall on it. "**It's useful not just for healing, but for reducing food and water intake. While in the trance I can go significantly longer without food or water... I've even heard of those in the Trance being able to withstand long durations without air.**"
**"Excellent skill, I hope you don't mind if I appropriate it from your mind the next time we... meld in the Force."**
"**I could just teach you.**"
**"Interesting proposition... Now that I picture it, I'm *more* interested in how you plan to play teacher."**
"**Well... The way I was taught was being placed in the Trance by another and then after that it's just practice.**"
"**It's not too far of a stretch from meditation, it's just allowing yourself to go deeper into the flow of the Force.**"
**"I see... I think you missed my meaning entirely, let's forget about that, I have no interested in wasting this day on Jedi topics."**
Lexi looks pointedly at Wraith indicating she had known where his thoughts had been going. "**No philosophy today, then? Or are you too restless for intellectual pursuits?**"

He had been taken aback at her rebuke, he found that philosophy was typically best practiced in Nature, but somehow this tender scene was just as beautiful, if not more so. Smiling slyly, he raised one eyebrow lasciviously: **"Very well. We can speak of Jedi and Sith philosophy, but only so long as you agree to the terms of my Game. Let's make it interesting by putting some stakes down. Every time that Sith Philosophy defeats the Jedi View, you get to remove one additional article of clothing, for the rest of the day. Deal?"**
Lexi can feel her cheeks turning pink at his forwardness. "**The entire day seems a bit impudent... Besides, what do I get out of it if I win?**"
He had not bothered to even outline the contralateral victorious outcome on purpose.
**"Name your prize."**
**"Aboard the Chimaera, you had expressed a desire to know the Secrets of the Reapers."**
**"How about, I'll expose one of my choosing, every time your philosophy triumphs."**
She raises an eyebrow at him considering. "**One of your choosing... What's changed your mind in telling me about the Reapers?**"

**"I suppose, its to do with your moral code, you were vehement yesterday not to involve the murder of even your strongest adversaries in the context of our primary mission. If you were to go that far, to protect your enemies, I doubt this information would ever be used to harm your... allies, shall we say."**
"**It's still knowledge. There's power in it. Don't get me wrong, I'm glad you've decided to trust me, but you were quite certain that it was best I didn't know.**"
**"Such certainly was only due to my lack of trust, but overnight, I've reconsidered."**
"**Alright then. That will be my prize.**" She pauses to consider him a moment. "**It's your game, what will be our first topic?**"
**"Attachments."**
**"The Jedi forgo such things, depriving themselves of their own Humanity. Do you not agree?"**
**"The Sith only restrict their Passions for the sake of Stealth."**
**"It is clear which philosophy has the right of it."**

"**It's true we avoid attachments, however we still form bonds: friendship and even love. We renounce more the unhealthly attachments: the belief we possess another person. When you truly care about someone, you care about their welbnbeing and happiness. The wrong kind of attachment leads to jealousy, greed, the fear of loss. It's those attachments we train ourselves to let go of.**"

**"I see your point, it would be an admirable one, if it weren't so strongly off the mark. In the real world, people are selfish. That is the Truth of All Life. In order to thrive, one must place themselves ahead of others. To deny this unshakable fact, is to deny life itself. All attachments are selfish. The bonds of what you call love and friendship, are also ultimately selfish, in order to benefit a lack of one's desired social bond.
