The Droid's assessment of the situation was an understatement. The entire Bridgeway had been scattered by a massive explosion.
"**Sabotaged?**" She peers at the screen showing the smoking bridge. "**He's cut off our way to get to him... And trapped us with his creatures in the process.**"

Xod'mon appears on all the viewscreens at the same time: **"Hahaha~hello again, Wwwraithe~! How do you like my handiwork? I call it... Radical Redecorating~! HAHAHA-- And now, oh yes here we come to the best part... You see, I've always wanted to test my Homunculi Mark IV Horrorgaunts versus an S-Class Reaper. Place your bets now, ladies and gentlemen~!"**
Her resolute gaze comes to meet Wraith's. "**Does he ever shut up?**" she asks
The largest of the Viewscreens Cracks violently, as a massive monstrous claw pierces the wall to the Control Room.
His laughing image, mid laugh, is replaced by a Monster barreling through the wall.
**"Just keep him talking... When he does shut up, *that's* when things get dangerous."**

Lexi takes a surprised step back away, but then she gathers her calm and focus. She studies the creatures in front of her, much as she had with the Vornskr on Mykyr. Her mind reaches out to theirs through the Force.
Raises his red saber and begins squaring off with the First Intruder.

Lexi closes her eyes, forcibly calming her mind, and draws on the Force to cast a net of connections against the Three Alchemical Homunculi. At first, their minds seem impenetrable, but as Lexi dives deeper into the Spiritual Dimension she is able to touch the minds of the Sith Constructs. She recognizes that on the inside, they each house a human soul, trapped by Evil Sorcery, and unable to control their bodies, which have been warped by Alchemy and Torture, into these grotesque, horrid forms. Each of the minds she touches, emphatically imparts only one thing: *Please kill me*."

Lexi is filled with horror at the realization of what the Sith Alchemy has done to them. Beside Wraith she ignites her saber. "**These are no animals... They were sentients,**" She says evenly, her voice low. "**They want us to kill them.**"
Whilst Lt. Kai is preoccupied with blocking the flurry of attacks from the First Intruder, NZ has produced an electrostaff, engaged in acrobatic jump combat against the walls and columns in the room.
The Second Intruder knocks out another wall, claw missing NZ's agile form.
Last, but certainly not least, The Third Intruder faced its ugly maw directly at Lt. Ardena. This creature had an ugly Red Crest atop its forehead, pulsing with hatred.

Facing off against the corrupted being in front of her she reaches out, her free hand extending out with her thoughts. Whatever Alchemy had been performed on these poor souls was allowing her to reach out to them. Their anger and hatred sweeps over her once more as she touches their minds. At first their hatred threatens to overwhelm her as they converge on her and her companions. Hatred and pain, their souls cried out to her and it envelops her.   

She pushes through, the Force coming to her aid. “*Sleep… You can stop fighting now… Sleep,*” she beckons them, sending a calmness to overcome their pain. Their heads turn towards her as they process her command, their focus on her. “**Sleep**,” her voice comes out more forceful as her hand gently touches the claw of the red crested being in front of her. The posture of the homunculus changes as it hunches over and peers at Lexi. The others, following the same command focused on them by Lexi’s intent gaze, retreat from their targets, and begin to hunker down, nearly collapsing into a pile in their desire to sleep. 

 When the last of the beings closes its eyes, Lexi releases her hold on their minds. The sudden weariness sends her head spinning and she drops to her knees. Her lightsaber disengages as it clatters to the duracrete beside her. Breathing heavily, she tries to clear her head, push past her weariness and bring her focus back to the control room. There was still a Sith Alchemist to deal with; the perpetrator of the atrocities she’d just bidden to sleep.

Her perceptions of the control come back to her in a rush. Lexi blinks through the haze and deeming they were now safe, at least for the time being, withdraws the medpac from her pouch and sets about treating the blaster wound at last. The coolness of the bacta patch on her shoulder comes as a relief as she shrugs her robe back into place over it. 
Looking over at Wraith, Lexi’s weariness is palpable. Falling back on her heels she shifts into a more comfortable sitting position and rests her lightsaber across her thigh. “**I’m going to leave it to you and NZ to figure out how to reach Xod’mon… I need to rest.** With his agreement, she closes her eyes and gets what rest she can.

Xod'mon's hateful visage appears on the single remaining, intact viewscreen in the Control Room: **"NooOOooOoo~! My Beautiful Creations! How could you! They were supposed to tear you apart limb from limb and rip you into strands of meaty ribbons like a child on Life Day Morning opening up long awaited birthday presents! And I was meant to laugh and enjoy, instead you've just ruined my beautiful, perfect plan! How *DARE--fzzz--*"** Wraith punches the viewscreen, putting his fist through it, then withdraws to allow his self-healing ability to close up the wound.

**"Too much viewscreen'll rot your brain."** As he stands pondering what to do next, he sees Lexi descend and enter a Trance, announcing she was entering a Rest. Turning around, he sees the Horrid Creatures are still sleeping soundly. If he left them alive, they would awaken when Xod'mon once again wrested control away from the sleeping Lexi. Igniting his lightsaber once more, he strolled over to begin to decapitate the 3 Sith Creations whilst they slept.
**"Query: Lieutenant, the Captain has entered Rest Mode. Am I to assume you will be taking the mantle of Acting Captain? In such case, I am awaiting orders."**
**"Find us a way into that Institute, N-Zee."**

**"Acknowleged: Sir, anything taking off from the Spaceport will likely trigger the auto-defense turrets. They're configured to never fire on their own base, but once the Deceptor leaves, it'll present itself as a target. I recommend we find a secondary passageway to the Institute. One might theoretically exist somewhere within the Mining Complex, deep beneath the surface, but that would mean having to enter Tartarus Base, which appears to be the Staging Point of Xod's Sith Constructs, and explore it to its fullest in order to find such a route. If you should run into another pack of Horrorgaunts, you cannot count on them deciding to take a convenient Lightsaber Nap again."**
**"If we find the Control Room in the Mining complex, can't you just download all the maps from their database?"**
**"Yes sir, but such a theoretical underground bridge would most likely be kept secret and away from commonly accessible log entries, even inside the Control Room."**
**"Then write a program to scan an incomplete system of passageways in order to estimate where the bridge would most likely be, and we can head there."**
**"I am unable to comply."**
**"Of course, I should have known..."**

Lexi wakes from her Trance, her head much clearer from the healing rest. The first thing she notices is the decapitated remains of the homunculi she had left to sleep. Their fate had perhaps been unavoidable but it still left a profound sadness within her. Now perhaps they would find the peace they no longer had in life. 

She doesn't comment to Wraith, as she goes to join him and NZ at the console. Looking over their shoulder she takes in what they're looking at. "**Is there a plan?**"

Wraith grimaces, **"There is a *Sort of*a Plan. N-Zee thinks the Mining Complex may hold access to the Institute, given that it extends into the Planet for kilometers. Problem? We have no idea how to find it."**
**"The access point I mean."**
**"Plus, its clear from looking over holocram footage Xod has turned the Mining Complex into some kind of... Alchemical Mutant Menagerie, would be the only way to describe it."**
"**How large is the schism between the spaceport and the Institute? Perhaps there's suits we can use and go across that way.**"
**"That's another problem, the Imperial Institute has been armed with Turbolaser Turrets, anything walking out in the open will get vaporized instantly."**
"**And we can't just leave for the same reason. He'll just destroy the Antioch with us on it.**" She says thoughtfully.
**Yes we're effectively pinned down here unless we can break through on foot to the Institute."**
She gives Wraith a small smile. "**You're going to hate my idea.**" She pauses a moment. "**We let the Force guide us to the access point.**"
**"I *knew* you'd say those exact words."** He shakes his head, but there is a small smile at the corner of his eyes behind the Death Mask.
**"Query: What is, The Force?"**
**"It's not a concept Droids can understand, NZ. Just think of it as, Fate and Destiny, fused with Morality, then split into a Duality."**
**"Error: Unable to comply."**
**"Yep, I should have known..."**
With a grin of amusement, Lexi pulls out her commlink and opens the channel to Kryze. "**Kryze, how are the repairs coming?**"
**"Kss~shh~shk-- Kreeze here, repairs are under way. It's not pretty, but it doesn't need to be to still work, right? --Shhhk--"**
"**Indeed, not. We can worry about pretty later.  How long until completed?**"
**"I estimate 5 hours captain."**
Lexi winces at the amount of time needed. "**See what you can to speed that up. Our host has already blown up one part of this station, I wouldn't past him to take it all down.**"
She looks at Wraith and NZ as she turns off the commlink. "**Do we have enough information to make it to the tunnels in the mining facility or will we need to find a terminal there first?**"
**"According to this terminal, the tunnel entrance will be hard to miss, Captain."**
**"Roger, I'm working on it, over and out."**
"**Ok. Let's get a move on before Xod'mon decides to throw something else at us while we're sitting here.**"

**"Acknowledgement: Roger that, Captain. I will need to stay here if we want to retain control of the Droid Control Center. Or, do you wish me to instruct all Droids to self-terminate by assaulting the Homunculi? It may provide enough cover for us to be able to get into Tartarus undetected."**
"**Stay here for the time being and make sure the homunculi don't try to cut us off from the ship.  If we need you then we'll have you follow your plan.**'
**"Lastly, I recommend to depower all the holocams in the Spaceport under our control before we leave, so that our enemy cannot spy on our movements."**
**"Yes, Captain."**

A large Spacewalk Bridge connects the Spaceport to Mining Station Tartarus. The Bridge is better kept than the others, and is lined with advertisements for Czerka Corporation product lines of all kinds, from Investment Options, to Freighter Contracts, to Czerka Arms Weapons of all kinds.

The Bridge ducks underneath the surface by making a gradual slope, entering the Mining Complex from below. As you get closer, and progressively walk deeper, the place becomes significantly less inviting. You pass heaps of destroyed Destroyer Droids, ironically enough. They seem to have been little more than child's play at the hands of the Horrorgaunts, as Xod had called them. Deeper down, massive mucous strands line the ceiling and walls. It is unclear what type of creature, if any, produced these slick ropes of saliva.

Xeno-bestial noises abound, echoing from deeper within the Mining Compex. Everything from chittering, clattering, growling, hooting, hollering, baying and howling. There is cacophany of alien noises that sound pained, and highly unpleasant. There is a palpable air of fear and dread. Finally, up ahead you see the last of the Battle Droids, a group consisting of mostly B1, B2, and a few Destroyer Droids, at a small outpost, standing around and having a very strange argument with themselves about orders having been changed around too many times recently. NZ's voice comes out of the B1 Droid in the yellow B1 Battle Droid Commander's Color Scheme.
**"Attention Droids: Two Operators are approaching, follow their every command without question. Leader out, over."**

Lexi looks over the remaining group of droids dubiously. It was clear they weren't effective in countering the creatures coming from the mine, but perhaps they'd be able to provide a distraction. Stealth would only get them so far but hopefully far enough to where they'd be past the majority of the creatures.
"**Has there been any further attacks after the last three got past?**" She asks the leader of the droid squad.
**"Affirmative. Three More Large Beasts are guarding the Entryway to Tartarus Station. We are unable to dislodge them."**

Lexi turns to consider the entryway and the three creatures baring their path. She casts a sideways glance at Wraith. "**What do you think..  have the droids provide a distraction while we sneak in behind them? We can't fight our way the entire way through.**"

**"It could work, if the Droids draw all the attention and make a lot of noise, we can hide out behind some debris, until the Monsters charge the firing Droids to destroy them, while we sneak in behind the Sith Creatures, and enter the Mining Station."**

Lexi nods considering before turning to the droid commander and laying out a plan that would be to their advantage. "**Wait until we're in position before you attack,**" she says at last, pointing over to a series of debris that looked like a promising place for she and Wraith to lie in wait.
**"Calculating: I estimate the probability of success at 87%."**

The plan goes off without a hitch and the duo is able to finally enter the Main Mining Complex. The Main Warehouse area is a grotesque horror show of Crude Durasteel Cages filled to the brim with Monstrosities of Every Impossible Kind. Horrific Creations bearing nonsensical anatomy make up the bulk of the poor trapped souls, some not even possessing eyes or a mouth, laying starving or crying out in pain at never-healing sores. A never-ending cacophony of pain and suffering resounds. Lexi's Force sensitivities are overwhelmed by grief, terror, and utter despair. Wraith however, seems not to mind at all, remaining outwardly unaffected.
Outside, the sounds of battle resound as high-pitched B1 Battle Droids can be heard being smashed like toys by Three Bulky Beasts, **"No--nonono, wait that doesn't go there--"**

Turning her attention to the Main Warehouse Area, the duo leave the Battle Droids to their doomed fate. However, something begins to tug at Lexi's spirit. Above the din of xenobestial noises, her Force-sharpened senses can hear the distinct sobbing noises of a young female, somewhere in one of the innumerable cages around her.

Lexi is unprepared for the horror that greets them as the cross into the Alchemists menagerie. The tumult leaves her visablly shaking as she makes her way through and then suddenly she stops dead in her tracks. Her head swivels one way then another as she tries to locate the anomaly of the sobbing woman. She let's her senses and the Force guide her.
Closing her eyes, Lexi can see the vague green outline of a small female, in fetal position, sobbing quietly in a corner of one of the Cages.
Stepping away from Wraith she goes to find her. "**There's someone here,**" she says to him quietly in explanation, but doesn't give him the opportunity to protest as she seeks the prisoner out.

After walking down one of the Cage aisles, you finally spot the woman. She is gaunt, malnourished, draped in brown rags, and chained by her neck to the wall, thanks to a large metal shackle. She is sitting with her knees touching her chest. Her hands cover her face, while she sobs intermittently, shoulders bobbing up and down. You see she must be an alien, bearing two head-tails - probably Chagrian. The rest of her cage is filled with malformed creations too twisted by Alchemy, lacking eyes or senses, to even notice the female prisoner in the same vicinity.
**"Leave her, the Three Beasts could return any second."**
"**I'm aware, but I'm not leaving her to this hell.**" She looks at the cage to see if she'd be able to open it without alerting Xod she is doing so.
The Cages are low tech, bearing no wires, and probably could be sliced through with a lightsaber instantly.

Drawing out her lightsaber she cuts open the cage. She extinguishes her saber and heads slowly towards the sobbing woman so as not to startle her, but also very much aware they were low on time. She extends her thoughts towards the woman, sending calming impressions to soothe her.
The prisoner withdraws further, tightening up her whole body and tensing at Lexi's approach.
"**It's ok..**" Lexi says softly. "**We're not going to hurt you. I want to help you leave this place.**" Pointing at the collar she adds. "**I'm going to get that off of you, ok?**"
The Prisoner begins to whine, sobbing more uncontrollably, but doesn't resist as Lexi approaches.
Some of the quivering masses mixed with gristle, meat and bone begin to instinctively get excited at the cutting action of the lightsaber and the approach of a new fleshy thing into their cage.

"**Just hold still.**" Unable to get the actual collar off without potentially decapitating the woman, Lexi instead opts to cut the chain as close to the collar as she can. As the other forms around them grow excited she begins to hurry her movements and once the woman is free tries to get her out of the cage as quickly as she's able to.
**"Leave her, or we'll never make it."**
The Prisoner is shivering bodily, unable to even come to a standing position. The evil mis-shapen forms of Monsters approach ever closer.

Wraith darts in at the last second, slashing a few of the grotesque monstrosities away with his lightsaber, deactivating it, as he sweeps in, and picks up the new Prisoner into his arms, running out of the cage.

Lexi had been about to snap at Wraith, but he springs into action and aids her in getting the woman out of the cage. She removes her cloak and wraps it around the shivering woman while still in Wraith's arms. "**Ok.. let's go.**" Lexi looks about and reaches out with her senses.  She had said she'd let the Force guide her in finding their way and unless some obvious indicator made their way apparent, that's what she would do.

The four Jumbotron-sized displays near the ceiling of the Warehouse suddenly ignite, showing the wicked Visage of the Mad Alchemist, Xod'mon'orhkez. He laughs, beginning to monologue an evil speech to all his minions. Fortunately he seemed to be oblivious of the fact that Lexi and Wraith had already infiltrated his Monstrous Menagerie, but his quivering nasal voice was absolutely intent on sic'ing all the Creatures at the problem at once:
The Voice of the Mad Alchemist booms throughout the entire warehouse: **"Greetings and Salutations, my Wonderous Creations! It is I, your majestic benefactor, Doctor Xod."**
**"N-nno~!"** The Prisoner opens her eyes for the first time, removing her hands to show her true face, one of beauty marked by abuse.

Xod's demeanor changes from grandiose to spiteful in an instant: **"On your screen now, are 2 Force using individuals hiding out somewhere on Hyperion base. Those of my lovely minions who can find them first, kill them, and bring me their remains, shall find their Soul *released* from my tender ministrations. I wish for all of you, only the Best. Doctor Xod, out."**
The Prisoner blinks, looking back and forth between the Jumbotron and her two rescuers, blinking incredulously.
Lexi frowns at the theatrics and then casts a concerned glance at the woman. "**We can help you, but you have to let us.**"
**"M-my name is Vaz... Y-you're not with Doctor Xod... but then, Who are you?"**
All the Creatures, in All the Nearby Cages, increase their racket as they begin to crowd much closer to the Cages, straining to see Wraith and Lexi.

Turning her gaze to the creatures for a moment she frowns and motions for Wraith to start moving. "**My name is Lexi, and this Wraith. We're not with Xod. We are however trying to get to him so that we too may leave.**" As they move she pulls out one of her ration bars from a pouch. "**Here eat this. It should help give you some strength.**"

**"Th-thank you, I'm s-so hungry."** Her hands shake as she devours the ration bar. Meanwhile, the racket of Monstrous growls, roars, and screeches intensifies as more and more of the Sith Creations recognize the duo walking in their midst. Some of them begin to charge at their own cages, leading some of the cages to sway make metallic groaning noises of their own.
"**Vaz...**" she starts, increasing her pace beside Wraith. She pulls out her canteen next and hands it to the woman. "**Do you know any of these tunnels?**"

**"Y-yes, wh-why? Don't tell me you're lost?"** Each of the Monstrous Cages are now bobbing up and down all around them, metal bars jumping and distorting every which way. An echoing clattering noise is suddenly heard deep down in the Warehouse, as if one of the cages finally came loose.
"**The sooner we get away from this area the better. We also need you to lead us to where Xod is,**" Lexi says calmly.

Finishing the canteen, she wipes her mouth with the back of her hand, halting at Lexi's statement, also hearing the clanking echoing noise further afield: **"Oh-Oh-kay, let me think--"** She raises her head up, cautiously, and seemingly seeing past the Monsters as if they weren't there, seems to manifest determination in her eyes: **"That way!"** She points down one of the passageways.
Wraith, still carrying the Prisoner in his arms, locks eyes with Lexi, nods, and takes off in a dead heat down the tunnel Vaz had indicated.
At the same time, several Monsters have jumped atop one of the Cages, the outline of the the Creatures spiked and vicious.
Raising their Snouts into the Sky, the leader emits a high pitched Howl-Screech.
Lexi races to keep up with the taller and stronger Sith. She ignites her saber as she spots the creatures. As they pass into the tunnel she looks for some means of blocking it.
Agile forms flit and bound behind them, as multiple forms of Monstrosities give chase to the sprinting pair.

The tunnel is long and winding, carved out of the rocky dirt of Planet Hyperion. As they desperately try to sprint forward, Lexi spots a potential opportunity. Ahead, there is an industrial Excavator with its raised Bucket filled with Rocks to the brim. If she were to swipe with her lightsaber, as she passes, the Rocks could potentially bury their pursuers, or at least slow them down.

Seeing the opportunity to at least buy them some breathing space, she focuses her attention on the excavator and its load of debris. Timing it just right as she passes, she swings at it with her ignited saber.

The verdant green saber slices through the arm of the Excavator, initiating a raining shower of boulders in Lexi's wake. Just as one of the Homunculi was about to pounce onto the Jedi, it is intercepted mid-jump by a falling Stone the size of a fridge. The Creatures behind it find similar fates, but more importantly, the falling rocks block off the small side tunnel Vaz had directed Wraith to take, completely blocking out the Alchemical Monsters from being able to follow them any further.
Coming up short and stopping to catch his breath, Wraith sets Vaz down, his form leaning against one of the support beams, breathing hard, as Lexi jogs to catch up with them.
**"Nicely, -huff-huff-, done."**
Lexi comes up beside him, likewise winded by their run through the tunnels. "**Thanks...**" She huffs, grabbing at the stitch in her side. "**I.. think. We've lost them .**"
Finally catching her breath she turns her attention to the woman. "**Thank you, Vaz. How much further till we get to Xod? Do you know if there are  going to be any more creatures in our way?**"

**"M-m-Master Xod's Office is located up ahead, but its quite far. There's a whole Medical Center at the end of this tunnel. Within, there are high tech Homunculi that are even more dangerous than those we just faced, for different reasons entirely. I--I used to work there, until he went Crazy and decided he didn't need us anymore, except for to use as Test Subjects..."*** She shivers, withdrawing into her folded arms.
Crouching down next to Vaz she places a comforting hand on the woman's arm and sends soothing impressions through the Force. "**What's special about the homunculi in the med center?**" Lexi coaxes.

**"They're dubbed Advanced Prototypes, those who made it past the initial random mutation stages, having survived with at least a mind not warped by insanity. They're capable of instinctive reasoning and a few, even rational thought. This, combined with their incredible physical abilities, makes them... well, much more dangerous than just wild beasts."**

Lexi nods and hands Vaz another ration bar as she thinks. "**Are there any sort of service tunnels or shafts we can use to get around without the homunculi seeing us? Or perhaps somewhere we can bait them to and trap them?**" Lexi's thoughts a whirlwind as she considers their options. "**We'll also need an access card so we can start taking control of the Institute from Xod.**"

**"I can try to keep us moving along side corridors instead of the main thoroughfares, and if you happen to find a good Ambush Point, then a simple Flare of Force Power will probably attract them in droves... Hopefully. Also, we can try to use my Access Card, if it still works."**
Lexi looks up at Wraith before she stands up. "**We have a plan then.**" Her attention goes back to Paz and Lexi assesses her condition. "**Do you think you can walk?**"
Vaz shakes the dust off her knees, standing wobbly, **"Y-yes, I think so, I appreciate your risking your life to save me..."** He looks at Wraith, and ducks her head, blushing.
**"... Don't thank me. I would have left you there. Thank her."**
She turns to Lexi: **"You are a good hearted person, thank you."**
Lexi scowls at Wraith for a moment before giving Vaz a small smile. "**We wouldn't have gotten this far without you. Thank you.**"
**"I guess I should formally introduce myself..."** Courtesy. **"Doctor Vazz'ri De L'est, a pleasure to make your acquaintance. I owe you my life."**
Lexi gives the doctor a respectful Jedi bow. She does hesitate for a moment as she decides whether to introduce herself as a Jedi or just the Captain of the Antioch. "**Jedi Knight Lexi Ardena.**"
**"Wraith. Reaper S-Class."**
Seems quite shocked, scooting a little ways more away from Wraith, **"An... interesting duo."**
"**It has its moments,**" Lexi says without skipping a beat. "**Come we should go before Xod figures out what we're up to.**"
**"I'll lead the way."**
Lexi exchanges a glance with Wraith and then with a nod follows after Vaz.
Rolls eyes, then follows.

The group of three follow the dirty rocky tunnel for another half a click, after which there is an abrupt stop at a security door. Vaz presents her Security Chip, and the door indicator changes from red to green.
Inside, there is a flight of stairs running upward for several stories, and another security door permits entrance to the lowest level of the Moff Selindor Medical Research Institute.

At the Basement Level: The whole place has been turned upside down and redecorated in the most violent way possible. Massive blood smears line some walls. Glitchy electrical lighting strobes bright, hospital-like lighting. Random fleshy parts are scattered haphazardly, gurneys are overturned, and a few skeletons slump against walls - some human, and not.
Vaz holds her hands over her mouth, halting at the sight. **"I... I think I better let you lead from here..."**
Lexi looks around at the macabre scene, her lips pursed in a thin line. "**Which way?**" She asks after a long moment.
**"If I had to guess... Doctor Xod is probably in the Control Room at the Administrative Tower, at the farther end of this building."**
**"That or in the Laboratory Levels, 1 through 5, somewhere..."**
"**Ok . We'll head for the control room. If he's not there we may be able to locate him from there.**"

Ascending to Level 1, the trio sees much the same scene as below, with the addition of larger Viewscreens and Posters proclaiming the wonders of Medical Research that the Institute purports to be home to. In the Center of the Lobby, a massive statue of an Imperial Moff stands imperiously. One arm has crumbled and ugly malformed vines have grown around its ankles and knees. The face and torso has been smeared with purple blood, that spells out: *LIAR* As the trio approaches, a small recording plays, as a warm Imperial voice intones: **"Greetings Citizens of the Empire, my name is Moff Selindor, and I wish to personally welcome you to The Most Advanced Medical Research Facility in the Galaxy. Every branch of Imperial Reserach and Medicine is represented here, at the Empire's Leading Hospital and Research Center. In every aspect of this building, we spared No Expense. Rest assured your loved ones can rest easy in my care, and the care of my Wonderful Staff. Please, enjoy your stay."**
Her face looks downcast, **"I met the Moff on at least two occasions... He was a good hearted man, it would break his heart to see his dream reduced to this state."**
"**What happened? We thought to this place was abandoned?**"

**"The Empire happenned. The same as everywhere else. As soon as it became clear this place was meant to actually help people, it became a drain on the Empire's resources. Eventually, more ambitious Moffs had Selindor assassinated, took over his funding, and abandonned this entire planet."**

**"Back in its heyday, there was groundbreaking research going on here... Back then, I was just a young laboratory assistant. It was interacting with the staff and individuals here, seeing the look on the faces of the people who were healed here, that inspired me to dedicate my life to medical reserach, to finding cures to the Galaxy's worst ailments."**
Lexi nods as she looks around the ruined atrium. "**A nobel pursuit. Perhaps you'll be able to continue your work.**"

**"Back in those days, one of the Galaxy's leading Scientists was Doctor Xod'mon'orkhez, a true visionary and unrivalled master of medicine, it was said he hailed from the Unknown Regions. He was single-handedly responsible for coming up with some of the most ingenious cures ever devised, he must have saved millions of lives alone, probably more if you count his collaborations with his fellow researchers."**

**"As soon as funding began to dry up, he was forced to turn, thanks to The Empire, to military applications. He began to experiment with aliens first, then graduated to human beings. Each step stripped away more of his humanity, each day I saw him his eyes had become progressively more clouded..."**
Catching herself rambling, she gasps and looks at the duo, **"I'm sorry, please... forget I said anything. This place brings back bad memories..."**
**"That doesn't make sense, the Xod I knew was a Reaper, an artificial creation, how could he have been an actual person with a soul at some point in the past?"**
**"I... I don't know..."** She seems intimidated, and backs away scared.

"**It's alright, Vaz**" she says reassuringly and then looks to Wraith. "**You're clones so your genetic material had to have come from somewhere. Perhaps this is a cloned modified version of the Xod Vaz knew.**"
**"Ha-rumph... Perhaps... It would be just like the Empire to ruin a good man's reputation by naming a Reaper after him."**
**"Let's keep moving, this just makes me want to kill him all the more."**
Lexi takes the lead heading in the direction Vaz indicates is the best way to avoid the homunculi on the way to the control room.

On the 2nd floor, trio sees another gruesome mess of claw slashes across walls, bone and gristle fragments laying everywhere, slick pools of dried blood (some human, some alien, some neither), electrical short-circuiting bursts of electricity from broken halogen lights, and the smells of spilled hospital chemicals, as well as more skeletons laying in hospital beds in deathly repose, chained to their gurneys.
"**Where did all these beings come from?**" she says in a near whisper as each level brought to light more atrocities. "**How many levels are there?**" She asks more loudly to Vaz.

She seems to be more nauseous as she looks around, **"I dunno... but I do know I really don't want to be here. Above us, is the 2nd level, which is restricted as Biohazard Level 2. Above that, the third floor is Biohazard level 3, and so on. If there are any Homunculi being developed, they would most likely be in one of the restricted zones, so that Xod could access the most dangerous chemicals and compounds. I don't think I want to go up there, I'd rather hide out in one of the doctors offices or something... Those Monsters terrify me, and if we have to get into an encounter with those, Things, then I'll just be a liability to you. I'd rather find a nice office and lock myself in, hunker down, until I get the all clear. Don't worry, I know this place fairly well, including backdoors, so I think I can give our foes the slip if I'm discovered."**
**"Here, take my Keycard."**
She hands Lexi the Chit: **"You can use it to access the restricted areas."**

"**Yes, that would be the best course. Here take this commlink.**" Lexi pulls out the commlink she normally uses instead of the one built into her lightsaber. "**You can contact Wraith if you need to and we'll be able to reach you.**" Exchanging the devices she avoids looking around once more. "**Are there any maps we can access for the upper levels?**"
Nods, pointing at the wall, **"Visitor's Map."**
"**Right..**" Lexi heads over to the visitor map and downloads it onto her datapad. Looking it over she traces out the most advantageous path to the control room.
Lexi's smartsaber automatically maps the fastest route:
With the map and route up on her display Lexi turns it to show Wraith. "**Looks like we'll be going through the majority of the facility to get there.**"

Lexi's analysis was an understatement. Since the Turbolifts were all completely broken down, much like the rest of the facility, and a majority of the stairwells had collapsed, their route had them zig-zag through the maze of medical hallways and floors to their target goal. The first leg of the planned journey had them going through the First Floor until they reached the Help Desk, turning and walking up stairs to Level 2, doubling back and entering one of the other stairwells, to ascend to Level 3. Level 3 would have to be traversed all the way through the facility, finally ascending the next intact stairwell to Level 4, located in the farthest wing of the complex. Level 4 had double-high ceilings, and once again there would be some doubling back in order to find the next intact stairwell, which leads into Level 5. Level 5 looks to be the most ostentacious, with triple high ceilings and access to the Administrative Center, a standalone building within the complex itself. At the top of the Administrative Building, sprouting an Antenna Complex, is the Institute Control Room.

After memorizing their path, Lexi puts the map away and turns to Vaz. "**Go find your place to bunker down. We'll reach out when we've secured the facility or we're ready to leave.**" Her resolute gaze falls to Wraith, "**Let's go.**"
**"Good luck. Please... just don't leave me here all alone..."**
She gives you both once last glance over her shoulder, before setting out.
After she leaves, Wraith growls underneath his mask, **"Let's ditch her."**
**"I abhor weakness. Now that we're free of her baggage, I can really let loose... Xod, here we come."**

"**She's bought us as far as she should and she'll be safer here than where we're going,**" Lexi says in acknowledgment.  "**However I have to disagree. She may be physically weakened by what she's gone through, but mentally... It takes a strong being to come out of what she has and not be insane.**" Lexi looks up at Wraith. "**There's more than one kind of strength.**"

The First Floor yields no further obstacles, and the duo find themselves carefully navigating the crumbling stairs up to Biohazard Level 2. They are presented with a Checkpoint, this one was somehow intact and still functional, the door locked electronically. The Keycard is able to bypass it. Inside, the first chamber houses a slew of Biohazard Suits, complete with dome-shaped helmets and breathing apparatuses attached. It seems, Level 2 isn't as decrepit and destroyed like the rest of the building. Neat rows of Laboratory Benches, racks and racks of chemicals, as well as a complete Morgue facility, housing horrific nightmare corpses of all kinds.
Up ahead, a sign reads: *Biohazard Level 3, Warning: Live Organisms Ahead*
Lexi glances through the chemicals as they pass, looking for anything that might be useful as a weapon against the homuculi there were bound to come across.

Lexi can barely begin to comprehend what she's looking at, every detailed biochemical name is long, dense, and inscrutable. Wraith just shrugs, shaking his head too. After climbing another winding stairwell, and using the Keycard to get past yet another Checkpoint, the duo enters Level 3. Here, once again there is a semblance of Order. Giant Green Bacta tanks hold floating shady forms, bubbles rising steadily as the machinery hums. Viewscreens display vital signs, beeping indicating the sound of heartbeats, sometimes more than one heart in one tank, beating rapidly, or in some cases not at all.

The duo consults their map, realizing they will have to navigate a long, winding path along Level 3, in order to reach the far end of the medical complex, which apparently has a giant blue tank full of liquid, housing the Main Bacta Supply for the entire building.

Lexi peers into some of the bacta tanks as they move past, but she doesn't slow her pace as she makes her way to the next level, determined to find Xod and end is macabre expirimentations. "**This insanity has to be stopped,**" she says as they move though the level. "**Has he been like this since you've known him?**"

As if in answer to her question, at the flick of an unseen switch, the Viewscreens all change, displaying the snarling visage of the Mad Chiss Scientist. Xod's multiple mechanical voices echo down the Central Hallway along Level 3, electronic and at once ghost-like: **"End it? On the contrary, this is only the beginning... but where are my manners... Welcome welcome, my dear friends, please do make yourselves at home, welcome to my Laboratory, exactly where I intended to take you, though I had hoped to do so against your will, but oh well, water under the bridge my friends, you're here at last! Let me ask you something ..."** His expression grows distant, smiling widely, **"Why are some people born sickly, while others appear perfectly healthy? Why, are some born with the ability to Wield the Force, while others are left Forever in Darkness?"**

**"These are the questions I kept asking myself, over and over again, until I couldn't stand it anymore. I decided to dedicate my life to the Pursuit of Knowledge, ahead and above all other things. I eschewed morality, justice, dignity or conscience. I dedicated my life to one never-ending Knowledge Quest. Until, one day, I found the answers I so desperately sought."**

**"Thanks to my, shall we say, unfettered research... I was able to make more progress than any sentient has ever done. Effortlessly, I blew all the ancient theories out of the water. There is no single Force Gene, as previous generations of Scientists theorized. The Midichlorian Theory too, has been debunked: Midichlorians are not the Originators of Force Power, they are merely an outcome of individuals who hold within them, the Force."**

**"No, the Force is more than that, it is the Ability to Transcend Oneself, and tap into an Ancestral Genetic Legacy, encoded in the very genes that make up all of us, like the Blueprints of a Perfect Tower. Modernity has clouded the minds of sentients with its fake conception of the world, but the True Legacy of our Forebears lies hidden, dormant within our Genes, nay our very Souls. Each alien species has their own genetic legacy, and within each species there are clan bloodlines that comprise even more unique Force Signatures."**
Lexi continues moving through the third level, letting the scientist talk, Maybe Wraith was right and they needed to worry more when the man was quiet.

He sweeps his hands indicating his Laboratory Test Subjects: **"To discover the Origins of the Force, I had to get my hands dirty. Now my hands are covered in blood, but did I falter? Nay, I remained resolute in my Quest for Knowledge. I persevered despite all obstacles, and now, finally, I am closer to the Truth than ever before. My Last Apprentice abandoned me, but that just meant their heart and soul was not in their work. I am all that remains, and I am... all that I require."**

Blinking, he realizes that the duo seems to be ignoring him, and his visage becomes more furious, **"You think you're hunting me, but you have only entered my Domain, and become ensnarled in *my* trap. I know not how you got a hold of a working Keycard, but it doesn't matter. You've made a fatal error in coming here, my friends. Still... I always like to look on the Bright Side of Life. Here it is: now you'll have the *honor* of being the *first* to test my Advanced Prototype models."**

**"The Homunculus Mark V, otherwise known as the Ravenor-Class. Faster, stronger.... SMARTER. They are the Perfect Predator. Able to utilize uncanny Force abilities straight from birth. There is no more need for Training, Discipline, or Tradition. There is only the need for... Raw Genetic Material, and a dab of my very own... *Special Science*."**
Lexi pauses in her trek as Xod makes mention of his homunculi and glances about. If battle was to be iminent, a more strategic battleground was in order.
**"Allow me to introduce you... Voice Command: Unlock V9."**

With this command, one of the tanks begins to vent a gas rapidly, and the Bacta level drops low. You hear the breaking of glass, and barely register a blur as a hunched back, horrid form leaps into one of the shadows, gurgling a wretched, phlegm-filled high pitched growl. Lexi and Wraith attempt to find defensible ground, so that at least they cannot get surrounded.
**"Unlock V10 and V11."**
Two more tanks begin to drain, likewise producing the sounds of breaking glass.
**"What should we do? Run or fight?"**

At once, the 3 new predators raise their heads and immediately lock onto the two humans. This time, there is something New to their Presence in the Force. Their eyes pulse beastially, overflowing with the Dark Side. Their very being seems to radiate fear, loathing, hatred, and the yearning desire to Destroy All Life.

Lexi stands back to back with Wraith as the creatures emerge from their bacta enclosures. If these creatures were stronger than the ones they had encountered in the spaceport control room, their odds were not looking good.  The Dark Side radiating off them did nothing to assuage her concerns. "**Run,**" she says decisively and makes a break towards their destination, thowing odds and ends behind them using the Force to try and slow the creatures down.

So begins a hectic chase, the duo sprinting and hurdling over laboratory benches, bounding around tight corners, and launching themselves recklessly through doorways. All the while, the echoing laughter of Doctor Xod follows them at every step. The Ravenors keep pace, seemingly locked onto Lexi and Wraith's Force Signature, as no amount of running seems to be far enough away to lose their pursuers. The only thing that Lexi and Wraith have going for them is the fact that the Ravenors are still only now finding their first footing, warming up their muscles, and regaining their senses, but... they were awakening quickly, and now, as they were beginning to find traction, they were getting closer and closer. The Laboratory seemed never-ending, hundreds of hunched, floating forms suspended in bacta banks. They pass a massive tank of liquid, the Main Bacta Supply, and see a Massive Black Form floating within, not even wanting to know what the Titan could do if awakened. At last, the duo arrives at the stairwell indicated on their map, jumping through the doorway and dashing up the stairs. Ahead, they knew, would be another Checkpoint. Perhaps, if they were quick enough, they could use the Keycard to get past fast enough, and lock the creatures behind them on Level 3.

Lexi looks up at the stairwell, winding towards Level 4. She looks behind her to see the Ravenors around the corner, and then looks at Wraith, nodding. The two Force Jump up the stairwell and land at the Level 4 Checkpoint. Swiping their Keycard, they quickly lock the door behind them. A bulge in the metal door resounds as one of the Ravenors knocks into the thick durasteel door, but the barrier holds. The duo once more exchanged bewildered looks and sigh, shaking their heads in unison.

Level 4 is Botanical. Alien trees twist their way around the entire floor, each distinct and different, some growing flowers, others growing eyeballs. Alien insects, strange evil birds, and mutated rodents skitter underfoot. Jogging, the duo continue to follow the Smartsaber guidance.

Lexi, on edge from their experiences from the previous levels, casts furtive glances at everything as they pass. Aware that Xod could be listening, she instead looks over at Wraith with confusion at the botanical garden they had entered into. "**Do you think the door will hold them?**" she asks in a near whisper to her companion.

All of a sudden, the duo halt in their tracks as up ahead the small garden path is blocked by a new creature. This one is reptilian, feminine, and wielding a glowing sword, both weapon and wielder radiating the Dark Side. The alien hisses, **"sSssSSsnackssss... Going sSsssomewhere...?"** Behind the duo, a male Reptilian exits from behind a tree, lifting his own red glowing axe.
**"I'll take the one behind, you've got the female."**
Taking out his lightsaber he leaps at the axe-wielder, putting the force of all his arm muscles behind the initial blow.
Lexi acknowledges his command by drawing her lightsaber, the emerald blade nearly blending into the surounding flora. She takes up a defensive stance as she watches the alien in front of her.

Wraith locks sabers with the axe-wielding Serpentine Homunculus, somehow the red aura around the axe prevents the lightsaber from slicing through. Knocking heads, the Homunculus seems to be dazed for a split second, allowing Wraith to slice off one of its arms in a leap of disengaging fury.
Meanwhile, the Female Serpent Homunculus weaves her sword in a style of fighting Lexi has never seen, confusing Lexi and drawing a very painful wound out of the young Jedi.

Back on the other half of the battlefield, Wraith once again saber locks together with the male, this time, leaning in with all his weight, but... to his dismay the Snake-like Homunculus plants its feet and pushes back, overpowering the musclebound Sith. Angered at this development, Wraith Force pushes into their saber lock, the tip of the lightsaber poking through the Alien's head.

At the cat fight, Lexi finally learns the sequence of attacks from the female Homunculus, expertly dodging and slicing the head trail trailing behind the movements of the head, just by a split second. The Homunculus hisses, retreating up a nearby tree and remaining there, out of reach for now...

Lexi is unprepared for the speed in which the homonulus attacks with her blade, and it slices painfully into her side. Undaunted, Lexi regroups, gritting through the pain and executes a spin to get around the creature's next attack and lops off the headtail of the homunculus as it dodges out of the way of Lexi's lightsaber. Coming out of her turn, Lexi is suprised as her opponent retreats up the nearby tree.
Clutching at her bleeding side she looks up at the homunculus, her lips pursed in thought,
Wraith arrives to rejoin Lexi at her side, following her gaze he sees the hissing reptilian too now.
**"Are you alright? Can you keep going? We can't let them delay us."**

In the tree, you hear a pained reptilian cry as the creature clutches its own headtail, blood dripping down the branches of the tree, which awaken and begin to drink the blood up, strangely. Large mouths open, wooden teeth bear, and begin to eat the Homunculus one bite at a time.
Wraith winces, **"Uh, Ok... must admit, I did not see *that* coming."**

Lexi looks in shock as the tree consumes the homunculus and now concerned by her own bleeding wound presses harder to staunch the flow. She starts moving towards the exit, not wanting to be still with the man eating tree before them. "**I'll be fine. Let's try and find somewhere I can put a patch on.**"

The pair make it to the final stairwell, away from any carniverous trees, and stop to catch their breath. It is here they must make a difficult decision: should they stop and use the med kits, and risk being ambushed by Homunculi, or press forward without sitting still and letting the Monsters zero in on them.

Lexi turns away from Wraith as she grimaces in pain as she evaluates her wound. They didn't have the time to treat it properly. Not with the threat of more creatures after them. Turning to face him she makes a decision. "**We should keep going before he has a chance to regroup his creatures.**"

Running up the stairs, they find the last Checkpoint, and use the Keycard to enter Level 5. Glacing around, they breathe a sigh of relief as for the first time, there are no signs of any Laboratory Experiments. Instead, everywhere lay strewn about broken Air Ambulances, some tipped on their side, others remaining upright. Up ahead, past the Parking lot, stood the large building, and their target - the Administrative Center, and atop it, the Command and Control.
**"We're -huff-huff- Almost there..."**

Xod's face appears on the jumbo viewscreen above the entryway to the Administrative Building. His voice resounds from loudspeakers sitting atop lamp posts all across the plaza: **"Good... you made it, I was beginning to worry that you had been devoured too soon. Come come, you're finally ready Wraith, ready to know the Truth... The Final Reckoning is at hand. You shall, at last, learn, who you really are. Enter... if you dare."** The transmission ends, and the doors to the Building swoosh open.

Lexi's relief is palpable as she catches her breath and tries very hard to focus on the task at hand. She extends her senses into the surrounding area wondering if any homunculus lay in wait amongst the abandoned vehicles. Feeling safe for the moment, she leans against one of the tipped over ambulances, closing her eyes as she draws on the Force. 

Using her experience with the Trance and after seeing Wraith heal himself drawing on the power of the Force, she encourages the same healing technique. At first nothing happens. 

A surge of Force energy slams into her and she gives a small gasp at the suddenness. The pain in her shoulder and side recedes completely and she feels rejuvenated. As she opens her eyes they shine bright cyan for a moment. 

Lexi blinks up at the view screen on the administration building. A dread begins to grow in her at the Sith’s words, but it wasn't for herself she fears. Her gaze comes to rest on Wraith. While she wants to stop the Sith, there was some personal vendetta between the two of them. One that had yet to be explained in its entirety.

“**Do you know what he's going on about now?**” she asks, trying to get a gauge on the situation they are about to walk into.
Wraith had been nonplussed at the Alchemist's grandiose proclamations, **"No idea, he's probably taken too much of his own medicine, if you know what I'm sayin'."**

"**Right.**" Lexi turns towards the administration building. "**He certainly has a high opinion of his abilities and his creations.**" Taking a coming breath she looks over at Wraith and gives a nod. "**Let's finish this.**"
**"Let's, and then we can finally get off this Force-forsaken planet."** He balls his fists and strides confidently towards the open double doors to the Administrative Build.

**"Yes, please, do come in, I think you'll find it most... illuminating."** The lights on the First Floor come on brightly, as Wraith enters, revealing a clean and organized Medical Reception Area. Rows and rows of empty chairs stand idle. The First Floor is decorated as bright, happy, and optimistic - displaying the Wonders of Imperial Medicine. Prominently at one end, there is a sequence of Wall-sized Displays, which when taken together reveal a story - the story of how Dr. Xodmon won the Galen Erso Prize, the highest Scientific Accolade in the entire Galaxy. Only a handful of researchers have ever received one. The first recipient, Galen Erso, was the Designer of the Original Death Star - which ended all Galactic wars, and has since then kept peace and order throughout the Galaxy. Each and every researcher had made some kind of pivotal contribution to the success of Military Imperial Progress. **"During my career, I was renowned as the most brilliant scientific mind in the entire Galaxy. Everyone respected and revered the name... Xod. Hahahahahahhha~!"** Click-SHHHK. The jumbotron display shuts off, ending the transmission.

Lexi follows behind and she has to blink rapidly as she enters the near blindingly white reception room. Walking the length of the displays the question of how such an affluent and promising researcher could fall so far, just makes it even more forlorn.
Wraith stops at the final Wall Display, furrowing his brow. Pointing, he says, **"Hey, I recognize that symbol."**
Lexi stops to look over the symbol which has captured her companion's attention. "**From a mission?**"

Lexi regards the Final Wall Display. The 2.5D holoimage sees Doctor Xodmon, wearing a labcoat, shaking hands with Emperor Palpatine Reborn, as he is being presented The Galen Erso Prize. A crowd of scientists clap in unison in the foreground. Behind Doctor Xodmon, a massive Bacta Tank stands prominently, marked with a rather distinctive symbol.
Lexi instinctively realizes that she recognizes it too, having seen it somewhere before, on another Bacta Tank.

Lexi takes a surprised step back as she realizes the implications of what she is seeing and looks at the display for a date. At some point in time Xod'mon was instrumental in the cloning process which created Wraith.
The display reads: [ABY 4]

"**He knows... He knows about Mount Tantis or at least about the cloning process that created you.**" Her words come out shakily, almost hesitant. "**His research must be why there's so many clones with Force affinity.**"
Wraith narrows his eyes, index finger hovering lower to read the text underneath the Symbol. **"Reverse Engineered Advanced Prototype ExecutioneR."**
"**Reaper,**" Lexi says quietly. "**Five years ago he developed the process that created the Reapers.**"

Suddenly, the Holodisplay shuts off, smoothes out and is replaced by a Holo-Mirror, displaying Wraith staring back at himself in the mirror, except viewed in a black and white ghastly dimension as if through some Ghostly Mirror. The entire room goes dim, as slow cackling laughter builds, heard from the sole monitor remaining alight, which displays Xod's sparkling purple eyes and amused expression.
**"I promised you, that you would learn, who you really are, Brother."**
"**Enough of these games, Xod. You wanted Wraith here so cut to the chase: What do you want?**"
A single turbolift door slides open, filled with light, it stands stark against the darkened room.
**"Of course, I will explain everything."** The transmission cuts out.
Lexi turns to look at Wraith. "**I don't like this. It's like what Mira did all over again.**"
Wraith has been staring at his own ghostly duplicate, remaining still.
**"Reapers, we have to kill every one of them."**
**"Every one of... us."**

Seeing his intent focus on the reflection, Lexi interjects herself between the ghostly reflection. "**Wraith... Look at me.**" She urges with a gentle push in the Force to get his attention. "**We'll deal with Xod and then figure out what to do with the Reapers. We need to find Mount Tantis first and he may have the key to that knowledge.**" She gently places a hand on his arm. "**Whatever you think your biology accounts for doesn't define everything that your are.  You're not the same person that walked out of that portal 6 weeks ago.**"
Wraith does snap out of it, turning away and facing Lexi, the spell broken for the time being. **"That bastard is trying to get in my head, you're right. Let's go beat him to a pulp."**

Lexi nods, satisfied the spell had been broken. For the time being at least. "**Just remember: we're in this together.**" Pausing only a moment, she turns towards the turbolift and walks with Wraith towards the final leg  of this ordeal. "**Let's get some answers.**'
Lexi had of course seen, the Reaper symbol in her Brief Force vision, emblazoned upon Wraith's Cloning Tank. Now, it seems she was going to confront the origins of the R.E.A.P.E.R. Program in full.

Having mustered up enough courage, stepping into the Turbolift, Wraith and Lexi look into each other’s eyes as the door slides closed. Inside this medically sterile Turbolift, you feel yourself being lifted upwards as a Viewscreen Display switches on, showing a close up view of Xod’s Right Eye as it peers at you intently: **“You know you should give me more credit, I went out of my way to install a Special Visitor Aide inside my personal turbolift. Yes… this sedative Xyclon-3 gas will make you considerably more *pleasant* during your stay.”** Lexi feels her vision swimming, becoming suddenly dizzy and falling to the ground. Alongside her, Wraith slides down the wall of the turbolift, slumped over and unconsious. **”Shh shh shh… Just ease into it, there you go… Hushhhh, sleep my friends… the Doctor will see you now.”**

Wraith and Lexi slowly reawaken strapped inside individual Psychiatric Prisoner Restraint Beds, each tilted at 45 degree angles. These Extra secure Hospital Gurneys are made of thickened durasteel and are meant to hold only the most violent and dangerous psychopathic prisoners. Each includes bite-proof mouth covers, skull clamps, as well as Injection Port Wheels that clamp circumferentially around each elbow joint so as to more easily deliver sedative drugs, at the Doctor's Orders.

**"I promise to remove your mouth covers, if you promise not to bite your tongues out... just yet."** Xod glides into view, illuminated by a single light from above. He hits a button at one of the Port Wheels, and the mouth covers lift, allowing you to speak.
Wraith seems to be still too hazy to make sense of anything, and gurgles incoherently.

As the turbolift fills with the gaseous sedative, her head begins to swim. She tries to hold her breath and push past the sudden drowsiness, realizing the danger they are in. It's no use, however as the dosage must have been set for Wraith in mind. Her vision begins to blur and her head hurts at the effort.  She loses the battle and drifts off into oblivion.
She's not completely surprised when she awakens strapped down to some contraption. Their bodies confined to prevent them from hurting themselves and their 'caregivers'. She looks blearily at Xod, and she fights to bring her mind under her control from the influence of the sedative.

Lexi realizes, in the back of her mind, that these massive gurneys were meant to hold large violent males. Her small stature, thin arms, and waist however, might enable her to wiggle out where larger males would be unable to squeeze through.
Having examined Wraith, he casually strolls over to Lexi's side, wearing a lab coat and holding a single medical datapad in the crook of his elbow. **"And how is the patient feeling today?"**
"**Playing the good doctor?**" she asks groggily, the sarcasm thick.
**"Always."** He pats makes gestures with his hands, **"It says here in your chart that your name is listed as 'Jedi Knight Lexi Ardena,' care to explain how an extinct Jedi wound up on my planet?"**
Grinning, **"The Last Jedi died out nearly a decade ago. You accuse me of playing the Good Doctor, but seems you're playing... the Good Jedi."**

Lexi blinks at him and realizes he must have gotten the information from the identchip she carries from her time on the Chimaera. "**Not as extinct as the Emperor would hope. Even after all this time, small pockets of those who remember exist.**"
Muttering he glances over the datapad, **"Smaller and smaller every day, it would seem.."**

**"You Jedi are perpetually Stuck in the Past, trying to revive the glorious Old Republic, unable to step through and walk into The Brave New World we all find ourselves inhabiting. Don't worry, I'll help... ease you into the future of the Galaxy. It shall look very much like... well, what you already witnessed thus far I'm sure."**
From across the room, a rage filled howl erupts: **"Xod, I'm going to crush your skull like an egg--- AGghh!~!"**

Xod waggles a lazy finger: **"Ah ah ah, I wouldn't do that if I were you. Subsonic Resonators in the Skull Clamp automatically activate any time you engage the Force. They make treatment sessions much more... enjoyable."**
**"Now then, where were we? Ah yes, the supposed Jedi Knight... I believe you had some Questions for the Good Doctor, did you not? I bill by the hour, and I'm standing right here."**
**"I promised I would explain everything."**

"**You were involved in the Reaper program,**" she says at last her head finally clearing. "**You figured out how to reliably create Force Sensitive clones. Why go from creating life to corrupting it?**"

Chortling, he laughs briefly: **"Such a small minded child. Did you think that what you saw below classifies as corruption in your naive mind? Clearly, you've never been involved in any worthwhile scientific pursuit. Most experiments we run, ultimately fail. The key, is not to focus on the failures, but on the successes."**
**"Each and every one of my Creations is worthy, in my mind, no matter how malformed or distorted, for it gets me one step closer to success."**
'**Success towards what?**"
**"In order to understand that, you have to first understand the whole history of my body of research."**
**"You're not Doctor Xodmon, Xod, you're just a Reaper, just a cheap imitation Clone, don't try to take credit for work done by an infinitely better Scientist."**
Xod's calm doctorly demeanor cracked for the first time, one eyebrow arched menacingly, **"The patient will wait for the doctor in his bed, quietly."** He reingages the mouth cover over Wraith.

**"Nnnfff~!!"** The gag came over him, shutting off any more protests from Wraith. Lexi realized for the first time, that his Death Mask must have been somehow removed in the time they were unconscious.
**"That's better, now where were we..."**

**"Ah, yes. I was explaining the History of my Wonderous Accomplishments. You see it all began..."** The Mad Chiss begins to weave a long-winded explanation. To summarize... Dr. Xodmon, the Original Chiss Scientist, had created a Gene Therapy that would specifically Unlock and Repair One’s Genetic Heritage, leading to a powerful Clan Bloodline Force Ability to be restored inside of an individual’s DNA. However, this Gene Therapy had to be individually customized and tailored to the exact DNA sequence of an individual. The most time consuming process involved having to repair each sequence manually. In other words, it couldn’t be used for widespread population applications. Nevertheless, this was his primary contribution to Military Biowarfare Applications.
